premise: Statement1: Foster : many species of extremely large north american mammals became extinct during the last ice age, which was also the time of the first human migration to north america. Statement2: These species could not survive the dramatic changes wrought by this human migration. Statement3: Fisch : those extinctions were caused by the dramatic shift to a harsher climate. Statement4: The climate changed so rapidly that the species could not adapt.
hypothesis: Premise1: The huge size of the mammals made it difficult for them to migrate the great distances to milder environments.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Archaeological evidence reveals that the human migrants had a number of different kinds of large weapons.

premise: Statement1: Using fossil energy more efficiently is in the interest of the nation and the global environment, but major improvements are unlikely unless proposed government standards are implemented to eliminate products or practices that are among the least efficient in their class. Statement2: Objection : decisions on energy use are best left to the operation of the market.
hypothesis: Premise1: Often the purchaser of a product, such as a landlord buying an appliance, chooses on the basis of purchase price because the purchaser is not the person who will pay for energy used by the product.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: It would be unrealistic to expect society to make the changes necessary to achieve maximum energy efficiency all at once.

premise: Statement1: A recent study of 10,000 people who were involved in automobile accidents found that a low percentage of those driving large automobiles at the time of their accidents were injured, but a high percentage of those who were driving small automobiles at the time of their accidents were injured. Statement2: Thus, one is less likely to be injured in an automobile accident if one drives a large car rather than a small car.
hypothesis: Premise1: A large automobile is far more likely to be involved in an accident than is a small automobile.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Only a small percentage of those people involved in an automobile accident are injured as a result.

premise: Statement1: Adults have the right to vote ; so should adolescents. Statement2: Admittedly, adolescents and adults are not the same. Statement3: But to the extent that adolescents and adults are different, adults can not be expected to represent the interests of adolescents. Statement4: If adults can not represent the interests of adolescents, then only by giving adolescents the vote will these interests be represented.
hypothesis: Premise1: Adolescents should have their interests represented.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The right to vote is a right that all human beings should have.

premise: Statement1: Toxicologist : a survey of oil-refinery workers who work with mbte, an ingredient currently used in some smog-reducing gasolines, found an alarming incidence of complaints about headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath. Statement2: Since gasoline containing mbte will soon be widely used, we can expect an increased incidence of headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath.
hypothesis: Premise1: Headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath are among the symptoms of several medical conditions that are potentially serious threats to public health.

premise: Statement1: Last winter was mild enough to allow most bird species to forage naturally, which explains why the proportion of birds visiting feeders was much lower than usual. Statement2: The mild winter also allowed many species to stay in their summer range all winter without migrating south, thereby limiting the usual attrition accompanying migration. Statement3: Hence, last year's mild winter is responsible for this year's larger-than-usual bird population.
hypothesis: Premise1: Birds eating at feeders are more vulnerable to predators than are birds foraging naturally.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Birds that remain in their summer range all winter often exhaust that range's food supply before spring.

premise: Statement1: There is evidence to suggest that our cave-dwelling ancestors polished many of their flints to a degree far surpassing what was necessary for hunting purposes. Statement2: It seems, therefore, that early humans possessed an aesthetic sense.
hypothesis: Premise1: Flints were often used by early humans for everyday chores other than hunting.

premise: Statement1: Tea made from camellia leaves is a popular beverage. Statement2: However, studies show that regular drinkers of camellia tea usually suffer withdrawal symptoms if they discontinue drinking the tea. Furthermore, regular drinkers of camellia tea are more likely than people in general to develop kidney damage. Statement3: Regular consumption of this tea, therefore, can result in a heightened risk of kidney damage.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many people who regularly consume camellia tea also regularly consume other beverages suspected of causing kidney damage.

premise: Statement1: Editorialist : some people argue that we have an obligation not to cut down trees. Statement2: However, there can be no obligation to an entity unless that entity has a corresponding right. So if we have an obligation toward trees, then trees have rights. Statement3: But trees are not the sort of things that can have rights. Statement4: Therefore, we have no obligation not to cut down trees.
hypothesis: Premise1: Avoiding cutting down trees is not an obligation owed to some entity other than trees.

premise: Statement1: Mall owner : our mall's occupancy rate is so low that we are barely making a profit. On the other hand, a mall that is fully occupied costs about as much to run as one in which a rental space here and a rental space there stands empty. Statement3: Clearly, therefore, to increase profits we must sign up new tenants.
hypothesis: Premise1: The mall's operating costs could be cut by consolidating currently rented spaces in such a way that an entire wing of the mall could be closed up.

premise: Statement1: It was misleading for james to tell the core curriculum committee that the chair of the anthropology department had endorsed his proposal. premise: Statement1: The country of baurisia has, until now, been self-sufficient in both grain and meat. However, with growing prosperity in baurisia has come a steadily increasing per capita consumption of meat, and it takes several pounds of grain to produce one pound of meat. Statement2: Therefore, since per capita income in baurisia is almost certain to rise further but increases in domestic grain production are highly unlikely, baurisia is soon likely to become an importer of grain.

premise: Statement1: For most people, the left half of the brain controls linguistic capabilities, but some people have their language centers in the right half. Statement3: Therefore, people who have suffered a serious stroke on the left side of the brain without suffering any such impairment must have their language centers in the right half.
hypothesis: Premise1: If there are language centers on the left side of the brain, any serious stroke affecting that side of the brain damages at least one of them.

premise: Statement1: Economist : the most economically efficient way to reduce emissions of air pollutants is to tax them in proportion to the damage they are likely to cause. But in country y, many serious pollutants are untaxed and unregulated, and policy makers strongly oppose new taxes. Statement2: Therefore, the best way to achieve a reduction in air pollutant emissions in country y would be to institute fixed upper limits on them.
hypothesis: Premise1: Policy makers in country y do not oppose setting fixed upper limits on air pollutant emissions as strongly as they oppose new taxes.

premise: Statement1: Editorial in krenlandian newspaper : krenland's steelmakers are losing domestic sales because of lower-priced imports, in many cases because foreign governments subsidize their steel industries in ways that are banned by international treaties. But whatever the cause, the cost is ultimately going to be jobs in krenland's steel industry. Statement2: Therefore, it would protect not only steel companies but also industrial employment in krenland if our government took measures to reduce cheap steel imports.

premise: Statement1: Commemorative plaques cast from brass are a characteristic art form of the benin culture of west africa. Some scholars, noting that the oldest surviving plaques date to the 1400s, hypothesize that brass-casting techniques were introduced by the portuguese, who came to benin in 1485 a.d. but portuguese records of that expedition mention cast-brass jewelry sent to benin's king from neighboring ife. premise: Statement1: A physically active lifestyle has been shown to help increase longevity. premise: Statement1: Political analyst : several years ago, mcfarlane, the military dictator, had brooks, the former prime minister, arrested on charges of corruption. premise: Statement1: A scientist made three observations : ( 1 ) in the world's temperate zones, food is more plentiful in the ocean than it is in fresh water ; ( 2 ) migratory fish in temperate zones generally mature in the ocean and spawn in fresh water ; and ( 3 ) migratory fish need much nourishment as they mature but little or none during the part of their lives when they spawn. Statement2: On the basis of those observations, the scientist formulated the hypothesis that food availability is a determining factor in the migration of migratory fish. Statement3: Subsequently the scientist learned that in the tropics migratory fish generally mature in fresh water and spawn in the ocean.
hypothesis: Premise1: Whether in the tropics food is less plentiful in the ocean than in fresh water
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Whether in the world's temperate zones, the temperatures of bodies of fresh water tend to be lower than those of the regions of the oceans into which they flow

premise: Statement1: Linguist : regional dialects, many of which eventually become distinct languages, are responses by local populations to their own particular communicative needs. premise: Statement1: Offshore oil-drilling operations entail an unavoidable risk of an oil spill, but importing oil on tankers presently entails an even greater such risk per barrel of oil. premise: Statement1: By competing with rodents for seeds, black ants help control rodent populations that pose a public health risk. premise: Statement1: John looks like a professional bodybuilder. Statement2: He weighs 210 pounds and stands six feet tall, which is the size of an nfl linebacker. John looks huge when he enters the room. Statement3: Years of gym time have clearly paid off in spades.

premise: Statement1: Traditionally, students at kelly university have evaluated professors on the last day of class. premise: Statement1: Columnist : the amount of acidic pollutants released into the air has decreased throughout the world over the last several decades. premise: Statement1: Some people fear that global warming will cause the large ice formations in the polar seas to melt, thereby warming the waters of those seas and threatening the plankton that is crucial to the marine food chain. premise: Statement1: Politician : members of the national legislature have received a very large number of phone calls and letters from people wanting to express their opinions about the new bill before the legislature, which would increase the current tax on bidis and cigarettes by one rupee per pack to pay for new antismoking advertisements. premise: Statement1: Editorial : the city has chosen a contractor to upgrade the heating systems in public buildings. premise: Statement1: In an experiment, each volunteer was allowed to choose between an easy task and a hard task and was told that another volunteer would do the other task. Statement3: Most volunteers chose the easy task for themselves and under questioning later said they had acted fairly. But when the scenario was described to another group of volunteers, almost all said choosing the easy task would be unfair. premise: Statement1: Human beings have cognitive faculties that are superior to those of other animals, and once humans become aware of these, they can not be made happy by anything that does not involve gratification of these faculties.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many people familiar both with intellectual stimulation and with physical pleasures enjoy the latter more.

premise: Statement1: Some doctors believe that a certain drug reduces the duration of episodes of vertigo, claiming that the average duration of vertigo for people who suffer from it has decreased since the drug was introduced. premise: Statement1: Rapid population growth can be disastrous for a small city. premise: Statement1: Museum visitor : the national government has mandated a 5 percent increase in the minimum wage paid to all workers. premise: Statement1: Lawyer : juries are traditionally given their instructions in convoluted, legalistic language. premise: Statement1: Between 1980 and 2000 the sea otter population of the aleutian islands declined precipitously. hypothesis: Premise1: Most of the surviving sea otters live in a bay that is inaccessible to orcas.

premise: Statement1: Many economists claim that financial rewards provide the strongest incentive for people to choose one job over another. premise: Statement1: Paleontologists had long supposed that the dinosaur diplodocus browsed for high-growing vegetation such as treetop leaves by raising its very long neck. But now computer models have shown that the structure of diplodocus"s neck bones would have prevented such movement. The neck could, however, bend downward and even extend below ground level, allowing diplodocus to access underwater vegetation from dry land. premise: Statement1: Detective : people who repeatedly commit crimes like embezzlement or bribery without being caught tend to become more confident. premise: Statement1: Domesticated animals, such as dogs, have come into existence by the breeding of only the individuals of a wild species that are sufficiently tame. For example, if when breeding wolves one breeds only those that display tameness when young, then after a number of generations the offspring will be a species of dog. premise: Statement1: The minoan civilization flourished on the island of crete around 2000 b. ( c ) the discovery on crete of large numbers of bronze implements and the furnaces used in their manufacture shows that the minoans had a thriving bronze industry. premise: Statement1: One of the effects of lead poisoning is an inflammation of the optic nerve, which causes those who have it to see bright haloes around light sources. premise: Statement1: Novelists can not become great as long as they remain in academia. premise: Statement1: Researchers gave 100 first-graders after-school lessons in handwriting. Statement2: They found that those whose composition skills had improved the most had learned to write letters the most automatically. premise: Statement1: Numerous paintings and engravings representing warfare can be found in remains of all civilizations going back to and including the neolithic period, when agriculture was first developed. premise: Statement1: Researcher : the role of chemicals called pheromones in determining the sexual behavior of some animals is well documented. But, even though humans also produce these chemicals, it is clear that psychological factors have taken over this role in us. Statement2: Whereas for animals these behaviors are involuntary, which is a clear sign of chemical control, humans, by virtue of their free will, choose how they behave, and thus psychological factors take over. Statement3: So pheromones are merely a vestige of our evolutionary past.
hypothesis: Premise1: Voluntary action cannot have a chemical explanation
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Free will can be found only in humans

premise: Statement1: Commentator : the theory of trade retaliation states that countries closed out of any of another country 's markets should close some of their own markets to the other country in order to pressure the other country to reopen its markets. Statement2: If every country acted according to this theory, no country would trade with any other.
hypothesis: Premise1: For any two countries, at least one has some market closed to the other.

premise: Statement1: Most plants have developed chemical defenses against parasites. Humans ingest these natural pesticides without harm every day. premise: Statement1: Scientific and technological discoveries have considerable effects on the development of any society. premise: Statement1: Quillium is the most popular blood pressure regulating prescription drug on the market. premise: Statement1: One-year-olds ordinarily prefer the taste of sweet food to that of salty food. premise: Statement1: We are in a new industrial revolution that requires management trainees to develop" action learning" from real experience within business and industry, rather than getting tied up with theory and academia. premise: Statement1: Economist : our economy's weakness is the direct result of consumers'continued reluctance to spend, which in turn is caused by factors such as high-priced goods and services. premise: Statement1: Farmer : worldwide, just three grain crops-rice, wheat, and corn-account for most human caloric intake. premise: Statement1: Asbestos, an almost indestructible mineral once installed as building insulation, poses no health risk unless the asbestos is disturbed and asbestos fibers are released into the environment. premise: Statement1: Overexposure to certain wavelengths of strong sunlight is the main cause of melanoma, a virulent form of skin cancer. premise: Statement1: Essayist : one of the claims of laissez-faire economics is that increasing the minimum wage reduces the total number of minimum-wage jobs available. premise: Statement1: Emissions from automobiles that burn gasoline and automobiles that burn diesel fuel are threatening the quality of life on our planet, contaminating both urban air and global atmosphere. premise: Statement1: Most banks that issue credit cards charge interest rates on credit card debt that are ten percentage points higher than the rates those banks charge for ordinary consumer loans. hypothesis: Premise1: Two to three percent of the selling price of every item bought with a given credit card goes to the bank that issued that credit card.

premise: Statement1: Petrochemical industry officials have said that the extreme pressure exerted on plant managers during the last five years to improve profits by cutting costs has done nothing to impair the industry 's ability to operate safely. premise: Statement1: Private industry is trying to attract skilled research scientists by offering them high salaries. premise: Statement1: Teacher to a student : you agree that it is bad to break promises. premise: Statement1: Last year, mayor stephens established a special law-enforcement task force with the avowed mission of eradicating corruption in city government. premise: Statement1: Long-term and short-term relaxation training are two common forms of treatment for individuals experiencing problematic levels of anxiety. premise: Statement1: There is no reason why the work of scientists has to be officially confirmed before being published. premise: Statement1: The public is well aware that high blood cholesterol levels raise the risk of stroke caused by blood clots. premise: Statement1: Deep tillage is even more deleterious to the world's topsoil supply than previously believed. premise: Statement1: Psychologists recently conducted a study in which people from widely disparate cultures were asked to examine five photographs. premise: Statement1: Critic : it has been argued that the real value of a product is not equal to the price people are willing to pay for it, on the grounds that this price often exceeds the cost of the labor required to create it, and the excess goes to the investor as an unjustifiably acquired profit. Statement2: But since the existence of the product is as dependent on the investor's initial capital outlay as on the laborer's toil, this argument is fallacious.

premise: Statement1: Citizens of parktown are worried by the increased frequency of serious crimes committed by local teenagers. In response, the city government has instituted a series of measures designed to keep teenagers at home in the late evening. premise: Statement1: Legislator : we should not waste any more of the taxpayers'money on the government's job-creation program. Statement2: The unemployment rate in this country has actually risen since the program was begun, so the program has clearly been a failure.

premise: Statement1: Company president : most of our best sales representatives came to the job with a degree in engineering but little or no sales experience. premise: Statement1: In western economies, more energy is used to operate buildings than to operate transportation. Much of the decline in energy consumption since the oil crisis of 1973 is due to more efficient use of energy in homes and offices. Statement3: Since energy savings from these efficiencies save several billion dollars per year today, we can conclude that 50 to 100 years from now they will save more than $ 200 billion per year ( calculated in current dollars ).
hypothesis: Premise1: Technology used to make buildings energy efficient will not become prohibitively expensive over the next century.

premise: Statement1: Logging industry official : harvesting trees from old-growth forests for use in manufacture can reduce the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, since when large old trees die in the forest they decompose, releasing their stored carbon dioxide. Statement2: Harvesting old-growth forests would, moreover, make room for rapidly growing young trees, which absorb more carbon dioxide from the atmosphere than do trees in old-growth forests.
hypothesis: Premise1: Much of the organic matter from old-growth trees, unusable as lumber, is made into products that decompose rapidly.

premise: Statement1: Ancient humans in eastern north america hunted mammoths until the mammoth disappeared from the area around 13,000 years ago. hypothesis: Premise1: The engraving was made during the time when the settlement was occupied

premise: Statement1: Some members have criticized the club's president for inviting dr. hines to speak at the annual awards banquet without consulting other club members beforehand. So the current president acted appropriately in the way in which she invited dr. hines. Statement2: But a few years ago the previous club president hired a tax accountant even though he had not discussed it with club members beforehand.

premise: Statement1: Since empathy is essential for people to be willing to follow moral codes that sometimes require them to ignore their own welfare to help others, civilized society could not exist without empathy.
hypothesis: Premise1: Civilized society can exist only if there are people who are willing to at least sometimes ignore their own welfare to help others.

premise: Statement1: In an experiment, some volunteers were assigned to take aerobics classes and others to take weight-training classes. Statement2: After three months, each performed an arduous mathematical calculation. Just after that challenge, the measurable stress symptoms of the volunteers in the aerobics classes were less than those of the volunteers in the weight-training classes. Statement3: This provides good evidence that aerobic exercise helps the body handle psychological stress.
hypothesis: Premise1: On average, the volunteers assigned to the aerobics classes got a greater amount of aerobic exercise overall during the experiment, including any exercise outside the classes, than did the volunteers assigned to the weight-training classes.

premise: Statement1: A mathematical theorem proved by one mathematician should not be accepted until each step in its proof has been independently verified. Statement2: Computer-assisted proofs generally proceed by conducting a vast number of calculations -- surveying all the possible types of instances in which the theorem could apply and proving that the theorem holds for each type. Statement3: In most computer-assisted proofs there are astronomically many types of instances to survey, and no human being could review every step in the proof. Statement4: Hence, computer-assisted proofs involving astronomically many types of instances should not be accepted.
hypothesis: Premise1: The use of an independent computer program does not satisfy the requirement for independent verification of each step in a proof that is extended enough to be otherwise unverifiable.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Computers cannot be used to assist in generating proofs of mathematical theorems that involve only a very limited number of steps.

premise: Statement1: The master plan for the new park calls for the planting of trees of any species native to this area, except for those native trees that grow to be very large, such as the cottonwood. Statement3: Thus, the donated trees are probably consistent with the master plan.
hypothesis: Premise1: Three rivers nursery does not sell any tree species that grow to be very large.

premise: Statement1: In a study of patients who enrolled at a sleep clinic because of insomnia, those who inhaled the scent of peppermint before going to bed were more likely to have difficulty falling asleep than were patients who inhaled the scent of bitter orange. Statement2: Since it is known that inhaling bitter orange does not help people fall asleep more easily, this study shows that inhaling the scent of peppermint makes insomnia worse.
hypothesis: Premise1: The patients who inhaled the scent of bitter orange were, on average, suffering from milder cases of insomnia than were the patients who inhaled the scent of peppermint.

premise: Statement1: Sociologist : many rural residents claim to only approve of modest lifestyles ; yet, many of these same rural residents often buy magazines focused on the lives of celebrities. Statement2: However, it is proven that humans are often fascinated by the very things they condemn. hypothesis: Premise1: Most rural residents who buy the magazines focused on celebrities do not regard the celebrity lifestyles as modest.

premise: Statement1: Last year, pharmaceutical manufacturers significantly increased the amount of money they spent promoting new drugs, which they do mainly by sending sales representatives to visit physicians in their offices. Statement2: However, two years ago there was an average of 640 such visits per representative, whereas last year that figure fell to 501. sent3: so the additional promotion must have been counterproductive, making physicians less willing to receive visits by pharmaceutical sales representatives.

premise: Statement1: To decrease the number of crimes in city y, the city's police commissioner proposed taking some police officers from low-crime districts of the city and moving them to high-crime districts of the city. Statement2: His proposal is based on city y crime data that show that the number of crimes in any district of the city decreases when additional police officers are moved into that district.
hypothesis: Premise1: Districts of the city from which police officers are removed do not experience significant crime increases shortly after the removal of those officers.

premise: Statement1: Historian : fifteenth-century advances in mapmaking contributed to the rise of modern nation-states. Statement2: In medieval europe ( from the fifth to the fifteenth century ), sovereignty centered in cities and towns and radiated outward, with boundaries often ambiguously defined. Statement3: The conceptual shift toward the modern state began in the late fifteenth century, when mapmakers learned to reflect geography accurately by basing maps on latitude-longitude grids. Statement4: By the midseventeenth century, nearly all maps showed boundary lines.
hypothesis: Premise1: During the fifteenth century in europe, mapmakers simplified the borders of sovereignty by drawing clear lines of demarcation between political powers.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Borders did not become codified in europe until certain treaties were signed in the early nineteenth century.

premise: Statement1: A person reading a new book for pleasure is like a tourist traveling to a new place. Statement2: The reader reads, just as the tourist travels, to enlarge understanding rather than simply to acquire information. Statement3: Thus, it is better to read fewer books and spend more time on each rather than to quickly read as many as one can, just as it is better to travel to fewer places and spend more time in each rather than to spend a small amount of time in many different places.
hypothesis: Premise1: Tourists gain much more understanding of a place once they have spent several days at that place than they do in their first few days there.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Tourists typically learn something about the places they visit even when they are there only to relax.

premise: Statement1: In the united states, of the people who moved from one state to another when they retired, the percentage who retired to florida has decreased by three percentage points over the past ten years. Statement2: Since many local businesses in florida cater to retirees, this decline is likely to have a noticeably negative economic effect on these businesses.
hypothesis: Premise1: The total number of people who retired and moved to another state for their retirement has increased significantly over the past ten years.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The number of people who move out of florida to accept employment in other states has increased over the past ten years.

premise: Statement1: Cognitive scientist : using the pioneering work of comparative psychologist gordon gallup as a model, several studies have investigated animals'capacity for mirror self-recognition ( msr ). Statement2: Most animals exposed to a mirror respond only with social behavior, such as aggression. However, in the case of the great apes, repeated exposure to mirrors leads to self-directed behaviors, such as exploring the inside of the mouth, suggesting that these animals recognize the reflection as an image of self. Statement3: The implication of these studies is that the great apes have a capacity for self-awareness unique among nonhuman species.
hypothesis: Premise1: If an animal does not have the capacity for msr, it does not have the capacity for self-awareness.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Animals that do not exhibit msr may demonstrate a capacity for self-awareness in other ways.

premise: Statement1: Despite increasing international efforts to protect the natural habitats of endangered species of animals, the rate at which these species are becoming extinct continues to rise. Statement2: It is clear that these efforts are wasted.
hypothesis: Premise1: Species that would have become extinct have been saved due to the establishment of animal refuges.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Scientists estimate that at least 2000 species become extinct every year.

premise: Statement1: Genuine happiness consists not in pleasurable feelings but instead in one's sense of approval of one's character and projects. Statement2: Thus the happy life, in fact, tends to be the good life, where the good life is understood not -- as it usually is these days -- as a life of material well-being but rather as a morally virtuous life.
hypothesis: Premise1: People who approve of their own character and projects tend to lead morally virtuous lives.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Attaining happiness is the real goal of people who strive for material well-being.

premise: Statement1: Safety consultant : judged by the number of injuries per licensed vehicle, minivans are the safest vehicles on the road. Statement2: However, in carefully designed crash tests, minivans show no greater ability to protect their occupants than other vehicles of similar size do. Statement3: Thus, the reason minivans have such a good safety record is probably not that they are inherently safer than other vehicles, but rather that they are driven primarily by low-risk drivers.
hypothesis: Premise1: Minivans generally have worse braking and emergency handling capabilities than other vehicles of similar size.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Minivans tend to carry more passengers at any given time than do most other vehicles.

premise: Statement1: Essayist : when the first prehistoric migrations of humans from asia to north america took place, the small bands of new arrivals encountered many species of animals that would be extinct only 2,000 years later. Statement2: Since it is implausible that hunting by these small bands of humans could have had such an effect, and since disease-causing microorganisms not native to north america were undoubtedly borne by the new arrivals as well as by the animals that followed them, these microorganisms were probably the crucial factor that accounts for the extinctions.
hypothesis: Premise1: Very few species of north american animals not hunted by the new arrivals from asia were extinct 2, 000 years after the first migrations.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Individual humans and animals can carry a disease-causing microorganism without themselves suffering from the disease.

premise: Statement1: One test to determine whether a person has been infected with tuberculosis consists of injecting the person with proteins extracted from the tuberculosis bacterium. Statement2: Once a person has been infected by a bacterium, the person's immune system subsequently recognizes certain proteins present in that bacterium and attacks the bacterium. This recognition also takes place in the test and results in a skin irritation at the injection site. Statement3: Hence the physicians who designed the test reasoned that anyone who reacts in this manner to an injection with the tuberculosis proteins has been infected with tuberculosis.
hypothesis: Premise1: The ability of the proteins present in the tuberculosis bacterium to trigger the skin irritation is exclusive to that bacterium.

premise: Statement1: The makers of activite, a natural dietary supplement, claim that it promotes energy and mental alertness. Statement3: Clearly, activite must be effective, since otherwise it would not be in the company's interest to make such an offer.
hypothesis: Premise1: The makers of activite charge a handling fee that is considerably more than what it costs them to pack and ship their product.

premise: Statement1: To cut costs, a high school modified its airconditioning system to increase its efficiency. Statement3: This shows that a decrease in humidity can make people ill.

premise: Statement1: Early in the twentieth century, lake konfa became very polluted. Statement2: Recently fish populations have recovered as release of industrial pollutants has declined and the lake's waters have become cleaner. Statement3: Fears are now being voiced that the planned construction of an oil pipeline across the lake's bottom might revive pollution and cause the fish population to decline again. premise: Statement1: Certain groups of asian snails include both" left-handed" and" right-handed" species, with shells coiling to the left and right, respectively. Statement2: Some left-handed species have evolved from right-handed ones. Statement3: Also, researchers found that snaileating snakes in the same habitat have asymmetrical jaws, allowing them to grasp right-handed snail shells more easily. Statement4: If these snakes ate more right-handed snails over time, this would have given left-handed snails an evolutionary advantage over right-handed snails, with the left handed snails eventually becoming a new species. Statement5: Thus, the snakes'asymmetrical jaws probably helped drive the emergence of the left-handed snail species.
hypothesis: Premise1: Experiments show that the snail-eating snakes in this habitat fail more often in trying to eat left-handed snails than in trying to eat right-handed snails.

premise: Statement1: Economist : the wages of many of the lowest-paid corporate employees in this country would be protected from cuts by enacting a maximum wage law that prohibits executives at any corporation from earning more than, say, 50 times what the corporation's lowest-paid employees in this country earn. A maximum wage law would remove this incentive for these executives to cut the wages of their lowest-paid employees. Statement2: Currently, some executives try to increase corporate profits -- and their own salaries -- by cutting the pay and benefits of their corporations'employees.

premise: Statement1: Readers give more credibility and respond more positively to negative restaurant reviews if the critics begin their reviews by discussing the things they enjoyed about the restaurant. premise: Statement1: Data from satellite photographs of the tropical rain forest in melonia show that last year the deforestation rate of this environmentally sensitive zone was significantly lower than in previous years. premise: Statement1: During its caterpillar stage, the leopard magpie moth feeds on a plant called the natal grass cycad and by so doing laces its body with macrozamin, a toxin that makes the moth highly unpalatable to would-be predators. premise: Statement1: Everyone likes repertory theater. Statement2: Actors like it because playing different roles each night decreases their level of boredom. hypothesis: Premise1: The outlay of $10. 00 for the purchase of 9 fare tokens would not be prohibitive for bus riders who are poor.

premise: Statement1: Columnist : over the last 20 years the demand in north america for japanese-made automobiles has increased, whereas the japanese demand for north american-made automobiles has been stagnant. premise: Statement1: The vomeronasal organ ( vno ) is found inside the noses of various animals. premise: Statement1: Journalist : the new mayor is undeniably bold. Statement2: His assertions are made with utter certainty and confidence. Statement3: While these kinds of assertions may make him popular with the public, they also demonstrate that he is not an introspective person.

premise: Statement1: A smoker trying to quit is more likely to succeed if his or her doctor greatly exaggerates the dangers of smoking. premise: Statement1: Mayor : false alarms from home security systems waste so much valuable police time that in many communities police have stopped responding to alarms from homes whose systems frequently produce false alarms. premise: Statement1: It is undeniable that regular consumption of fast food can lead to serious health problems. premise: Statement1: Physician : in an experiment, 50 patients with chronic back pain were divided into two groups. premise: Statement1: High schools should only teach abstinence. Statement2: Students who practice abstinence will never experience an unplanned pregnancy or contract a sexually-transmitted disease.

premise: Statement1: Environmentalist : when bacteria degrade household cleaning products, vapors that are toxic to humans are produced. premise: Statement1: Compared to us, people who lived a century ago had very few diversions to amuse them. Statement2: Therefore, they likely read much more than we do today.

premise: Statement1: Although withholding information from someone who would find that information painful is sometimes justified, there is no such justification if the person would benefit from having the information. "

premise: Statement1: The five senses have traditionally been viewed as distinct yet complementary. premise: Statement1: College is increasingly unaffordable for everyone that is not independently wealthy, outpacing inflation every year since the early 1970s. premise: Statement1: Environmentalist : an increased number of oil spills and the consequent damage to the environment indicate the need for stricter safety standards for the oil industry. In particular, the government has to at least require oil companies to put double hulls on their tankers and to assume financial responsibility for accidents. premise: Statement1: After the second world war, the charter of the newly formed united nations established an eleven-member security council and charged it with taking collective action in response to threats to world peace. Statement2: The charter further provided that the five nations that were then the major powers would permanently have sole authority to cast vetoes. alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Decisions reached by a majority of nations in response to threats to world peace would be biased in favor of one or more major powers

premise: Statement1: Until he was dismissed amid great controversy, hastings was considered one of the greatest intelligence agents of all time. Soon after the dismissal, however, it was shown that he had never been incompetent. Statement2: It is clear that if his dismissal was justified, then hastings was either incompetent or else disloyal. Statement3: Thus, one is forced to conclude that hastings must have been disloyal.
hypothesis: Premise1: Hastings's dismissal was justified.

premise: Statement1: The press reports on political campaigns these days as if they were chess games. Statement2: One candidate's campaign advisor makes a move ; the other candidate's advisor makes a countermove. Statement3: The press then reports on the campaign advisors and not on the candidates. Statement4: The losers in this chess game are the voters. Statement5: They are deprived of the information they need to make informed decisions because the press is ignoring substantive policy issues and reporting only on the process of the campaign. Statement6: It is clear that the campaign advisors should stay out of the limelight and let the press report on the most revealing positions on substantive issues the candidates have taken.
hypothesis: Premise1: The candidates in the election are taking positions on substantive policy issues.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: There is no difference between reporting on the political process and reporting on substantive issues.

premise: Statement1: Tiger sharks are common in the waters surrounding tenare island. Statement2: Usually tiger sharks feed on smaller sharks, but sometimes they have attacked tourists swimming and surfing at tenare's beaches. Statement3: This has hurt tenare's tourism industry, which is second only to its fishing industry in annual revenues. Statement4: In order to help the economy, therefore, the mayor of the island has proposed an ongoing program to kill any tiger sharks within a mile of the beaches.
hypothesis: Premise1: The small sharks on which tiger sharks prey feed on fish that are commercially important to the island's fisheries.

premise: Statement1: Columnist : shortsighted motorists learn the hard way about the wisdom of preventive auto maintenance ; such maintenance almost always pays off in the long run. In hiring this adviser, the council made an investment that is likely to have a big payoff in several years. hypothesis: Premise1: The columnist's city has a much smaller population and economy than the other cities did when they began devoting resources to economic development planning.

premise: Statement1: Moralist : immoral actions are those that harm other people. Statement2: But since such actions eventually harm those who perform them, those who act immorally do so only through ignorance of some of their actions'consequences rather than through a character defect.
hypothesis: Premise1: Those who, in acting immorally, eventually harm themselves do not intend that harm.

premise: Statement1: Mayor : the city council has proposed additional funding for the town library. The city council claims that the library is underutilized because the books, electronic media, and computer equipment are outdated, and that if these were updated, more residents would use the library. premise: Statement1: Microbiologist : a lethal strain of salmonella recently showed up in a european country, causing an outbreak of illness that killed two people and infected twenty-seven others. Investigators blame the severity of the outbreak on the overuse of antibiotics, since the salmonella bacteria tested were shown to be drug-resistant. Statement2: But this is unlikely because patients in the country where the outbreak occurred can not obtain antibiotics to treat illness without a prescription, and the country 's doctors prescribe antibiotics less readily than do doctors in any other european country.
hypothesis: Premise1: People in the country where the outbreak occurred often consume foods produced from animals that eat antibiotics-laden livestock feed.

premise: Statement1: Patti : most parents are eager for their preschoolers to learn as much as possible. Statement2: However, instead of providing general opportunities for their children to learn, parents often direct their children's learning to their own personal concerns. Statement3: Because children have a natural curiosity and thirst for knowledge, they learn an enormous amount simply through growing and adapting to the world. Statement4: Therefore, this type of directed learning is unlikely to improve a child's preschool education.
hypothesis: Premise1: The type of directed learning in question is not a necessary part of the process of growing and adapting to the world.

premise: Statement1: In 1860 bavarian quarry workers discovered the impression of a feather in a limestone slab dating to the mesozoic era. But there in limestone lay the imprint of a fully aerodynamic, three-inch-long feather. Statement2: It had previously been assumed that birds developed only after the close of the mesozoic era and after the disappearance of pterosaurs, a species characteristic of that era. hypothesis: Premise1: In the mesozoic era, no creatures other than birds had such feathers.

premise: Statement1: In a certain democratic country the legislature passed a new tax law over the principled objections of the parliamentary opposition. Statement2: Some opposition leaders broke the new law by refusing to pay the new tax. They defended their lawbreaking by citing the historical precedent in the country of activists'having to break laws in winning for women the right to vote.
hypothesis: Premise1: The opposition leaders, unlike the activists, were part of the democratic process that they are defying.

premise: Statement1: Columnist : much of north america and western europe is more heavily forested and has less acid rain and better air quality now than five decades ago. Statement2: Though this may be due largely to policies advocated by environmentalists, it nonetheless lends credibility to the claims of people who reject predictions of imminent ecological doom and argue that environmental policies that excessively restrict the use of natural resources may diminish the wealth necessary to adopt and sustain the policies that brought about these improvements.
hypothesis: Premise1: Nations sustain their wealth largely through industrial use of the natural resources found within their boundaries.

premise: Statement1: An overly centralized economy, not the changes in the climate, is responsible for the poor agricultural production in country x since its new government came to power. Statement2: Neighboring country y has experienced the same climatic conditions, but while agricultural production has been falling in country x, it has been rising in country y.

premise: Statement1: Critic : works of literature often present protagonists who scorn allegiance to their society and who advocate detachment rather than civic-mindedness. However, modern literature is distinguished from the literature of earlier eras in part because it more frequently treats such protagonists sympathetically. Statement2: Sympathetic treatment of such characters suggests to readers that one should be unconcerned about contributing to societal good. hypothesis: Premise1: It is to the advantage of some individuals that they be concerned with contributing to societal good.

premise: Statement1: The profitability of a business is reduced by anything that undermines employee morale. Statement2: This is why paying senior staff with stock options, which allows them to earn more when the enterprise prospers, is not a wise policy because it increases dramatically the difference in income between senior staff and employees who are paid only a fixed salary.
hypothesis: Premise1: Large income differences between fixed-salary employees and senior staff tend to undermine employee morale.

premise: Statement1: Harry trevalga : you and your publication have unfairly discriminated against my poems. premise: Statement1: It is widely assumed that people need to engage in intellectual activities such as solving crossword puzzles or mathematics problems in order to maintain mental sharpness as they age. In fact, however, simply talking to other people -- that is, participating in social interaction, which engages many mental and perceptual skills -- suffices. Statement2: Evidence to this effect comes from a study showing that the more social contact people report, the better their mental skills.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many medical conditions and treatments that adversely affect a person's mental sharpness also tend to increase that person's social isolation.

premise: Statement1: A recent epidemiological study found that businesspeople who travel internationally on business are much more likely to suffer from chronic insomnia than are businesspeople who do not travel on business. International travelers experience the stresses of dramatic changes in climate, frequent disruption of daily routines, and immersion in cultures other than their own, stresses not commonly felt by those who do not travel. Statement2: Thus, it is likely that these stresses cause the insomnia.

premise: Statement1: The everyday behavior of whales is particularly difficult to study because introducing novel stimuli, such as divers or submarines, into the whales'environment causes whales to behave in unusual ways. Statement2: Some biologists plan to train sea lions to carry video cameras on their backs and, on command, to swim along with whales. premise: Statement1: Science fiction creates an appetite for interstellar space exploration among certain people. Unfortunately, this appetite can not be satisfied with any technology humanity will soon possess. premise: Statement1: Public health will improve more quickly in the wake of new medical discoveries if medical researchers abandon their practice of waiting until their findings are published in peer-reviewed journals before informing the press of important research results. premise: Statement1: Efforts to get the public to exercise regularly, which have emphasized the positive health effects of exercise rather than the dangers of a sedentary lifestyle, have met with little success. premise: Statement1: The number of serious traffic accidents ( accidents resulting in hospitalization or death ) that occurred on park road from 1986 to 1990 was 35 percent lower than the number of serious accidents from 1981 to 1985. sent2: the speed limit on park road was lowered in 1986. sent3: hence, the reduction of the speed limit led to the decrease in serious accidents.

premise: Statement1: Radio stations with radio data system ( rds ) technology broadcast special program information that only radios with an rds feature can receive. premise: Statement1: For years, university administrators, corporations, and government agencies have been predicting an imminent and catastrophic shortage of scientists and engineers. premise: Statement1: Several recent studies establish that most people would want to be informed if they had any serious medical condition. Statement2: In each study, over 80 percent of the people surveyed indicated that they would want to be told.

premise: Statement1: Tiger beetles are such fast runners that they can capture virtually any nonflying insect. However, when running toward an insect, the beetles intermittently stop, and then, a moment later, resume their attack. Statement2: Perhaps they can not maintain their pace and must pause for a moment's rest ; but an alternative hypothesis is that while running tiger beetles are unable to process the resulting rapidly changing visual information, and so quickly go blind and stop.

premise: Statement1: Appliance dealer : appliance manufacturers commonly modify existing models without giving the modified versions new model names. premise: Statement1: In a study conducted in pennsylvania, servers in various restaurants wrote" thank you" on randomly selected bills before presenting the bills to their customers. premise: Statement1: Manufacturers of writing paper need to add mineral" filler" to paper pulp if the paper made from the pulp is to look white. Statement3: Therefore, barring the more efficient use of fillers in paper manufacturing or the development of paper-whitening technologies that do not require mineral fillers, if writing paper made from recycled paper comes to replace other types of writing paper, paper manufacturers will have to use more filler than they now use.
hypothesis: Premise1: Grayish writing paper will not be a universally acceptable alternative to white writing paper.

premise: Statement1: Acme corporation offers unskilled workers excellent opportunities for advancement. premise: Statement1: Computer manufacturers have sought to make computer chips ever smaller, since decreasing the size of a computer's central processing unit ( cpu ) chip -- without making that cpu chip any less sophisticated -- will proportionally increase the speed of the cpu chip and the computer containing it. premise: Statement1: Commentator : many people argue that the release of chlorofluorocarbons into the atmosphere is harming humans by damaging the ozone layer, thus allowing increased amounts of ultraviolet radiation to reach earth. Statement2: Because the supernova's disruption was much greater than the estimated effect of chlorofluorocarbons today, there is no reason to think that these chemicals in the atmosphere harm humans in this way.

premise: Statement1: In his book, published in 1892, grey used the same metaphor that jordan used in her book, which was published in 1885. the metaphor is so unusual that there is little chance that two different people independently created it. premise: Statement1: Native speakers perceive sentences of their own language as sequences of separate words. This is shown by the fact that travelers who do not know a local language hear an unintelligible, uninterrupted stream of sound, not sentences with distinct words. premise: Statement1: Even if many more people in the world excluded meat from their diet, world hunger would not thereby be significantly reduced.

premise: Statement1: Half a dozen q40 airliners have crashed in the past two years. Statement2: The manufacturer has countered claims that the q40's design is faulty by pointing out that, in each of the accidents, investigations indicated pilot error as the cause.

alt_hypothesis: Premise2: One of the crashes occurred in very poor visibility when the pilot was attempting to land.

premise: Statement1: Journalist : in physics journals, the number of articles reporting the results of experiments involving particle accelerators was lower last year than it had been in previous years. Statement2: Several of the particle accelerators at major research institutions were out of service the year before last for repairs, so it is likely that the low number of articles was due to the decline in availability of particle accelerators.

premise: Statement1: A study of kindergarten students has shown that children from households that own many books, an average of 54 books, show average or high reading achievement levels, while the households of children with lower reading achievement levels tend to own fewer books, 5 books on average.
hypothesis: Premise1: When children have books of their own, they develop more advanced reading skills because adults are more likely to read to them regularly.

premise: Statement1: Over 40,000 lead seals from the early byzantine empire remain today. Statement2: Apart from the rare cases where the seal authenticated a document of special importance, most seals had served their purpose when the document was opened. Statement3: Lead was not expensive, but it was not free : most lead seals would have been recast once they had served their purpose. Statement4: Thus the number of early byzantine documents sealed in such a fashion must have been many times the number of remaining lead seals.
hypothesis: Premise1: Most of the lead seals produced during the early byzantine empire were affixed to documents that were then opened during that period.

premise: Statement1: Tv meteorologist : our station's weather forecasts are more useful and reliable than those of the most popular news station in the area. The same can not be said for either of our competitors.

premise: Statement1: A scientific team compared gold samples from several ancient artifacts with gold samples from an ancient mine in western asia. The ratios of the trace elements in these samples were all very similar, and they were unlike the trace-element ratios from any other known mine. Statement2: It is therefore likely that the gold in the artifacts was dug from the ancient mine.

premise: Statement1: During a press conference, council member johns stated that the advisory council appointed by mayor smith has been one of the least effective in recent history. premise: Statement1: A recent study suggests that alzheimer's disease, which attacks the human brain, may be caused by a virus. Statement4: This led the scientist who conducted the study to conclude that alzheimer's disease might be caused by a virus.

premise: Statement1: Between 1951 and 1963, it was illegal in the country 25. sent2: of geronia to manufacture, sell, or transport any alcoholic beverages. Statement2: Despite this prohibition, however, the death rate from diseases related to excessive alcohol consumption was higher during the first five years of the period than it was during the five years prior to 1951. sent4: therefore, the attempt to prevent alcohol use merely made people want and use alcohol more than they would have if it had not been forbidden.

premise: Statement1: Bacteria that benefit human beings when they are present in the body are called commensals. premise: Statement1: The familiar slogan" survival of the fittest" is popularly used to express the claim, often mistakenly attributed to evolutionary biologists, that the fittest are most likely to survive.
