premise: Statement1: Even though trading in ivory has been outlawed by international agreement, some piano makers still use ivory, often obtained illegally, to cover piano keys. Statement2: Recently, experts have devised a synthetic ivory that, unlike earlier ivory substitutes, has found favor with concert pianists throughout the world. Statement3: But because piano makers have never been major consumers of ivory, the development of the synthetic ivory will therefore probably do little to help curb the killing of elephants, from whose tusks most natural ivory is obtained.
hypothesis: Premise1: The most common use for natural ivory is in ornamental carvings, which are prized not only for the quality of their workmanship but also for the authenticity of their materials.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The new synthetic ivory can be manufactured to resemble in color and surface texture any of the various types of natural ivory that have commercial uses.

premise: Statement1: Rhizobium bacteria living in the roots of bean plants or other legumes produce fixed nitrogen, which is one of the essential plant nutrients and which for nonlegume crops, such as wheat, normally must be supplied by applications of nitrogen-based fertilizer. Statement2: So if biotechnology succeeds in producing wheat strains whose roots will play host to rhizobium bacteria, the need for artificial fertilizers will be reduced.
hypothesis: Premise1: Rhizobium bacteria living in the roots of wheat would produce fixed nitrogen.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Biotechnology should be directed toward producing plants that do not require artificial fertilizer.

premise: Statement1: During the period in which there are no competitive races, two runners -- runners a and runners b -- take part in an experiment measuring their vo2 max, the volume of oxygen an athlete can use. Statement2: During these sessions, the runners engaged in moderate aerobic activity, or a sustained heart rate between 146-154 beats per minute. Statement3: At the end of the sessions, runner a had a greater vo2 max than did runner b. therefore, once the two runners begin identical intensive training -- sessions involving over 168 beats per minute-- for the race season, runner a will continue to have the greater vo2 max, assuming that neither become injured and that both train with similar intensity.
hypothesis: Premise1: Regarding their vo2 maxes, runners respond equally to intensive training.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The amount one trains does not influence one's vo2 max.

premise: Statement1: In a town containing a tourist attraction, hotel and restaurant revenues each increased more rapidly over the past year than did revenue from the sale of passes to the attraction, which are valid for a full year. Statement2: This led those in charge of the attraction to hypothesize that visitors were illicitly selling or sharing the passes.
hypothesis: Premise1: The local board of tourism reports that the average length of stay for tourists remained unchanged over the past year.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: While the cost of passes is unchanged since last year, hotel and meal prices have risen.

premise: Statement1: The retail price of decaffeinated coffee is considerably higher than that of regular coffee. However, the process by which coffee beans are decaffeinated is fairly simple and not very costly. Statement2: Therefore, the price difference can not be accounted for by the greater cost of providing decaffeinated coffee to the consumer.
hypothesis: Premise1: The beans used for producing decaffeinated coffee do not cost much more before processing than the beans used for producing regular coffee.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Processing regular coffee costs more than processing decaffeinated coffee.

premise: Statement1: In modern deep-diving marine mammals, such as whales, the outer shell of the bones is porous. Statement2: This has the effect of making the bones light enough so that it is easy for the animals to swim back to the surface after a deep dive. Statement3: The outer shell of the bones was also porous in the ichthyosaur, an extinct prehistoric marine reptile. Statement4: We can conclude from this that ichthyosaurs were deep divers.
hypothesis: Premise1: In most modern and prehistoric marine reptile species that are not deep divers, the outer shell of the bones is porous.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Some deep-diving marine species must surface after dives but do not have bones with porous outer shells.

premise: Statement1: A study of 8,000 randomly chosen adults in a european country found that 75 percent of those who consumed alcohol an average of twice or more per week over a six-month period suffered at least one cold during that period. Statement2: We can tentatively conclude from this that consuming alcohol tends to increase one's susceptibility to colds.
hypothesis: Premise1: Seventy-five percent of those in the study who never consumed alcohol suffered at least one cold over the six-month period.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Eighty percent of those in the study who consumed alcohol three times or more per week suffered colds over the six-month period.

premise: Statement1: Guidebook writer : i have visited hotels throughout the country and have noticed that in those built before 1930 the quality of the original carpentry work is generally superior to that in hotels built afterward. Statement2: Clearly carpenters working on hotels before 1930 typically worked with more skill, care, and effort than carpenters who have worked on hotels built subsequently.
hypothesis: Premise1: The better the quality of original carpentry in a building, the less likely that building is to fall into disuse and be demolished.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The materials available to carpenters working before 1930 were not significantly different in quality from the materials available to carpenters working after 1930.

premise: Statement1: Spectroscopic analysis has revealed the existence of frozen nitrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide on the surface of pluto. Statement2: Such ices have a tendency to vaporize, producing an atmosphere. Statement3: Since the proportion of any gas in such an atmosphere depends directly on how readily the corresponding ice vaporizes, astronomers have concluded that the components of pluto's atmosphere are nitrogen, carbon monoxide, and methane, in order of decreasing abundance.
hypothesis: Premise1: There is no frozen substance on the surface of pluto that vaporizes more readily than methane but less readily than carbon monoxide.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: There is no more frozen nitrogen on the surface of pluto than there is either frozen carbon monoxide or methane.

premise: Statement1: On average, corporations that encourage frequent social events in the workplace show higher profits than those that rarely do. Statement2: This suggests that the ez corporation could boost its profits by having more staff parties during business hours.
hypothesis: Premise1: The great majority of corporations that encourage frequent social events in the workplace do so at least in part because they are already earning above-average profits.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Frequent social events in a corporate workplace leave employees with less time to perform their assigned duties than they would otherwise have.

premise: Statement1: A study followed a group of teenagers who had never smoked and tracked whether they took up smoking and how their mental health changed. Statement2: After one year, the incidence of depression among those who had taken up smoking was four times as high as it was among those who had not. Statement3: Since nicotine in cigarettes changes brain chemistry, perhaps thereby affecting mood, it is likely that smoking contributes to depression in teenagers.
hypothesis: Premise1: Participants who were depressed at the start of the study were no more likely to be smokers after one year than those who were not depressed.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The study did not distinguish between participants who smoked only occasionally and those who were heavy smokers.

premise: Statement1: Waste management companies, which collect waste for disposal in landfills and incineration plants, report that disposable plastics make up an ever-increasing percentage of the waste they handle. Statement2: It is clear that attempts to decrease the amount of plastic that people throw away in the garbage are failing.
hypothesis: Premise1: An increasing proportion of the paper, glass, and metal cans that waste management companies used to handle is now being recycled.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Because plastics create harmful pollutants when burned, an increasing percentage of the plastics handled by waste management companies are being disposed of in landfills.

premise: Statement1: Sociologist : research shows, contrary to popular opinion, that, all other things being equal, most people who have pets are less happy than most people who do not. Statement2: Therefore, any person who wants to be as happy as possible would do well to consider not having a pet.
hypothesis: Premise1: Most people who have pets feel happier because they have pets.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: All people who have no pets admit to feeling unhappy sometimes.

premise: Statement1: Political scientist : the dissemination of political theories is in principle able to cause change in existing social structures. Statement2: However, all political theories are formulated in the educationally privileged setting of the university, leading to convoluted language that is alienating to many individuals outside academia who would be important agents of change. Statement3: It follows that, with respect to political theory, there is a special role for those outside the university context to render it into accessible, clear language.
hypothesis: Premise1: Persons within academic settings are less willing or less able than persons outside to write in a straightforward way.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Persons outside academic settings are the most important agents of change to the social structure.

premise: Statement1: In defending the hyperion school of journalism from charges that its program is of little or no value to its students, the dean of the school pointed to its recent success in placing students : 65 percent of its graduates went on to internships or jobs in print or broadcast journalism.
hypothesis: Premise1: More than half of the school's students came from jobs in journalism to improve their skills.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Some newspaper editors do not regard journalism schools as a necessary part of the training of a journalist.

premise: Statement1: The current pattern of human consumption of resources, in which we rely on nonrenewable resources, for example metal ore, must eventually change. Statement2: Since there is only so much metal ore available, ultimately we must either do without or turn to renewable resources to take its place.
hypothesis: Premise1: We cannot indefinitely replace exhausted nonrenewable resources with other nonrenewable resources.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: There are renewable resource replacements for all of the nonrenewable resources currently being consumed.

premise: Statement1: Recently discovered prehistoric rock paintings on small islands off the northern coast of norway have archaeologists puzzled. Statement2: The predominant theory about northern cave paintings was that they were largely a description of the current diets of the painters. This theory can not be right, because the painters must have needed to eat the sea animals populating the waters north of norway if they were to make the long journey to and from the islands, and there are no paintings that unambiguously depict such creatures.
hypothesis: Premise1: The cave paintings that were discovered on the islands depicted many land animals.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Parts of the cave paintings on the islands did not survive the centuries.

premise: Statement1: Economist : as should be obvious, raising the minimum wage significantly would make it more expensive for businesses to pay workers for minimum-wage jobs. Statement2: Therefore, businesses could not afford to continue to employ as many workers for such jobs. Statement3: So raising the minimum wage significantly will cause an increase in unemployment.
hypothesis: Premise1: Businesses typically pass the cost of increased wages on to consumers without adversely affecting profits.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most workers are earning more than the current minimum wage.

premise: Statement1: Regular weightlifting is necessary for good health. Statement2: Weightlifting with heavy resistance, especially with compound movements, helps break down and rebuild stronger muscle fibers, resulting in strength and size gains.
hypothesis: Premise1: Strength and size gains are indicators of good health.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Regular weightlifting is necessary for good health.

premise: Statement1: The level of triglycerides in the blood rises when triglycerides are inadequately metabolized. Research shows that patients with blood triglyceride levels above 1 milligram per milliliter are twice as prone to heart attacks as others. Statement2: Thus, it is likely that consuming large amounts of fat, processed sugar, or alcohol, each known to increase triglyceride levels in the blood, is a factor causing heart disease.
hypothesis: Premise1: Heart disease interferes with the body's ability to metabolize triglycerides.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Triglyceride levels above 2 milligrams per milliliter increase the risk of some serious illnesses not related to heart disease.

premise: Statement1: Critic : works of modern literature can not be tragedies as those of ancient playwrights and storytellers were unless their protagonists are seen as possessing nobility, which endures through the calamities that befall one. Statement2: In an age that no longer takes seriously the belief that human endeavors are governed by fate, it is therefore impossible for a contemporary work of literature to be a tragedy.
hypothesis: Premise1: Those whose endeavors are not regarded as governed by fate will not be seen as possessing nobility.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most plays that were once classified as tragedies were misclassified.

premise: Statement1: A professor of business placed a case-study assignment for her class on her university's computer network. She later found out that instead of reading the assignment on the computer screen, 50 out of the 70 students printed it out on paper. Statement2: Thus, it is not the case that books delivered via computer will make printed books obsolete.
hypothesis: Premise1: Studies consistently show that most computer users will print reading material that is more than a few pages in length rather than read it on the computer screen.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Scanning technology is very poor, causing books delivered via computer to be full of errors unless editors carefully read the scanned versions.

premise: Statement1: The city's transportation department has noticed that the city's extensive public bus system generally runs behind schedule on days when traffic is heavier in the city. Statement2: The transportation commissioner concluded that the traffic prevents the buses from reaching their stops on time, despite the existence of a number of bus-only lanes throughout the city. Statement3: He has therefore proposed creating additional bus-only lanes on city streets to allow more buses to bypass traffic.
hypothesis: Premise1: The city's bus-only lanes do not contribute substantially to gridlock by reducing the number of lanes available to other traffic.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The city owns enough buses to cover all established routes.

premise: Statement1: Patel : it is often thought that direct experience, unlike language and culture, can always serve as common ground for communication among human beings. Statement2: But people from different cultures inhabit different sensory worlds. Statement3: Because selective screening of sensory data admits some perceptions while filtering out others, one person's experience, perceived through one set of culturally patterned sensory screens, is quite different from what anyone from another culture would experience when encountering the same sensory data.
hypothesis: Premise1: No two people from different cultures have the same sensory screens.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Two people from the same culture sometimes have different sensory screens.

premise: Statement1: Politician : the cohesion of a society depends on its members'accepting a set of basic principles. When these principles are routinely called into question, the society begins to erode. Statement2: Any society, therefore, that allows schools to present the society's set of basic principles as simply one of several alternatives, with the suggestion that students may choose which to accept on the basis of the principles'worth, is inviting its own demise.
hypothesis: Premise1: Given the chance to assess the merits of the principles governing their societies, individuals will rarely find those principles acceptable.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Unless people believe that they have freely chosen to adopt the principles that govern their societies, they will tend to repudiate these principles.

premise: Statement1: In 1992 outlaw fishing boats began illegally harvesting lobsters from the territorial waters of the country of belukia. Soon after, the annual tonnage of lobster legally harvested in belukian waters began declining ; in 1996, despite there being no reduction in the level of legal lobster fishing activity, the local catch was 9,000 tons below pre-1992 levels. Statement2: It is therefore highly likely that the outlaw fishing boats harvested about 9,000 tons of lobster illegally that year.
hypothesis: Premise1: The illegal lobster harvesting was not so extensive that the population of catchable lobsters in belukia's territorial waters had sharply declined by 1996.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Outlaw fishing boats do not, as a group, harvest more lobsters than do licensed lobster-fishing boats.

premise: Statement1: Grow-again ointment is a proven treatment for reversing male hereditary baldness. Five drops daily is the recommended dose, and exceeding this quantity does not increase the product's effectiveness. Statement2: Therefore, offering a manufacturer's rebate on the purchase price of grow-again will not increase sales and consequently would be unprofitable for the manufacturer.
hypothesis: Premise1: The rebate, if offered, would not attract purchasers who otherwise might not use grow-again.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: When using an ointment, people tend to believe that applying it in greater quantities can make it more effective.

premise: Statement1: A favored theory to explain the extinction of dinosaurs, together with many other species, has been the globally catastrophic collision of a large asteroid with the earth. Supporting evidence is an extraterrestrial chemical element in a layer of dust found worldwide at a geological level laid down contemporaneously with the supposed event. Statement2: A new competing theory contends that any asteroid impact was irrelevant, because it was massive volcanic activity that caused the extinctions by putting enough dust into the atmosphere to cool the planet. Statement3: The deccan region of india contains extensive volcanic flows that occurred within the same time period as the supposed asteroid impact and the extinctions.
hypothesis: Premise1: The massive volcanic activity was not caused by the impact of an asteroid
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The extinctions took place over a longer time period than they would have if caused by the impact of an asteroid

premise: Statement1: In the past, when there was no highway speed limit, the highway accident rate increased yearly, peaking a decade ago. Statement2: At that time, the speed limit on highways was set at 90 kilometers per hour ( kph ) ( 55 miles per hour ). Every year since the introduction of the highway speed limit, the highway accident rate has been at least 15 percent lower than that of its peak rate. Statement3: Thus, setting the highway speed limit at 90 kph ( 55 mph ) has reduced the highway accident rate by at least 15 percent.
hypothesis: Premise1: Thanks to changes in automobile design in the past ten years, drivers are better able to maintain control of their cars in dangerous situations.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: It was not until shortly after the introduction of the highway speed limit that most cars were equipped with features such as seat belts and airbags designed to prevent harm to passengers.

premise: Statement1: Voters commonly condemn politicians for being insincere, but politicians often must disguise their true feelings when they make public statements. Statement2: If they expressed their honest views-about, say, their party 's policies-then achieving politically necessary compromises would be much more difficult. Statement3: Clearly, the very insincerity that people decry shows that our government is functioning well.
hypothesis: Premise1: Achieving political compromises is not all that is necessary for the proper functioning of a government.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Some political compromises are not in the best long-term interest of the government.

premise: Statement1: Professor robinson : a large meteorite impact crater in a certain region was thought to be the clue to explaining the mass extinction of plant and animal species that occurred at the end of the mesozoic era. Statement2: However, the crystalline structure of rocks recovered at the site indicates that the impact that formed this crater was not the culprit. Statement3: When molten rocks crystallize, they display the polarity of earth's magnetic field at that time. But the recrystallized rocks recovered at the site display normal magnetic polarity, even though earth's magnetic field was reversed at the time of the mass extinction.
hypothesis: Premise1: The crater indicates an impact of more than sufficient size to have caused the mass extinction.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The recovered rocks recrystallized shortly after they melted.

premise: Statement1: An independent computer service company tallied the service requests it receives for individual brands of personal computers. Statement2: It found that, after factoring in each brand's market share, krv brand computers had the largest proportion of service requests, whereas probit brand computers had the smallest proportion of service requests. Statement3: Obviously, probit is the more reliable personal computer brand.
hypothesis: Premise1: For some computer brands, but not for others, most service requests are made to the manufacturer's service department rather than to an independent service company.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The computer brands covered in the computer service company's tally differ greatly with respect to their market share.

premise: Statement1: The continents of south america and africa were once joined together as one landmass. Statement2: Evidence for this claim can be found in the fact that fossils of mesosaurs, extinct reptiles, have been unearthed in both western africa and eastern south america.
hypothesis: Premise1: Mesosaurs in africa migrated from europe and mesosaurs in south america migrated from asia.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The fossils of mesosaurs were found very near the west african and east south american coasts.

premise: Statement1: Researcher : any country can determine which type of public school system will work best for it by investigating the public school systems of other countries. Statement2: Nationwide tests could be given in each country and other countries could adopt the system of the country that has the best scores on these tests.
hypothesis: Premise1: A type of school system that works well in one country will work well in any other country.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: A number of children in each country in the research sample are educated in private schools.

premise: Statement1: Climate change is caused by an increase of carbon dioxide in the earth's atmosphere. Overall, deforestation has led to the loss of half the world's trees. Statement2: Carbon dioxide traps heat and remains in the atmosphere longer than other heat-trapping gases. Statement3: Between 2000 and 2012.890,000 square miles of trees around the world were cut down -- more than one-eighth of the original forest covering earth. Statement4: Unless the rapid pace of deforestation is halted, the earth's climate will change drastically in the near future.
hypothesis: Premise1: Trees lower the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Climate change negatively impacts the earth.

premise: Statement1: Engineer : thermophotovoltaic generators are devices that convert heat into electricity. The process of manufacturing steel produces huge amounts of heat that currently go to waste. Statement2: So if steel-manufacturing plants could feed the heat they produce into thermophotovoltaic generators, they would greatly reduce their electric bills, thereby saving money.
hypothesis: Premise1: The amount steel-manufacturing plants would save on their electric bills by feeding heat into thermophotovoltaic generators would be sufficient to cover the cost of purchasing and installing those generators.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Using current technology, it would be possible for steel-manufacturing plants to feed the heat they produce into thermophotovoltaic generators in such a way that those generators could convert at least some of that heat into electricity.

premise: Statement1: Over the last 25 years, the average price paid for a new car has steadily increased in relation to average individual income. Statement2: This increase indicates that individuals who buy new cars today spend, on average, a larger amount relative to their incomes buying a car than their counterparts did 25 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: Sales to individuals make up a smaller proportion of all new-car sales than they did 25 years ago.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Allowing for inflation, average individual income has significantly declined over the last 25 years.

premise: Statement1: Manager : one reason productivity in our office is not as high as it could be is that office workers spend too much time taking unauthorized breaks. Statement2: Since the number of office workers assigned to each manager will soon be reduced, managers will be able to supervise workers more closely in the future to make sure that they are not taking unauthorized breaks. Statement3: Therefore, productivity in our office will soon increase.
hypothesis: Premise1: The gain in productivity that will result from reducing unauthorized breaks will exceed any loss in productivity caused by closer supervision.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The best way to improve productivity is to reduce the number of employees and give more work to employees who remain on the job.

premise: Statement1: The existence of polluting industries near schools does not lead to a decrease in the air quality at those schools. Statement2: Proof of this is shown at middle school x in southern california, where an oil well was built on middle school x's campus. Statement3: The air quality at middle school x is no worse than the air quality at other middle schools in the state, and the students at middle school x do not suffer more frequently from air pollution-related allergies than do students in other middle schools in the state.
hypothesis: Premise1: More animal and plant life thrive near middle school x than at other middle schools in the state.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Middle school x, located near the oil well, is also farther from major highways and other sources of air pollution than the other middle schools in the state.

premise: Statement1: Lines can be parallel in a euclidean system of geometry. Statement2: But the non-euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification is regarded by several prominent physicists as correctly describing the universe we inhabit. Statement3: If these physicists are right, in our universe there are no parallel lines.
hypothesis: Premise1: There are no parallel lines in the non- euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most physicists have not doubted the view that the universe is correctly described by the non-euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification.

premise: Statement1: Alan : government subsidies have been proposed in cariana to encourage farmers in rochelle, the country's principal agricultural region, to implement certain new farming techniques. Statement2: Unless these techniques are implemented, erosion of productive topsoil can not be controlled. Statement3: Unfortunately, farmers can not afford to shoulder the entire cost of the new techniques, which are more expensive than those currently used. Statement4: Therefore, without subsidies, agricultural output in rochelle will inevitably decline. Statement5: Betty : but erosion in rochelle is caused by recurring floods, which will end next year once cariana completes the hydroelectric dam it is building across the region's major river. Statement6: Therefore, rochelle's total agricultural output will stabilize at its present level even without subsidies.
hypothesis: Premise1: The current yearly output, if any, from rochelle's land that will be permanently under water once the dam is completed will at least be matched by additional yearly output from rochelle's remaining land.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The government of cariana has sufficient financial resources both to subsidize its farmers' implementation of new farming techniques and to operate a hydroelectric dam.

premise: Statement1: Bram stoker's 1897 novel dracula portrayed vampires -- the" undead" who roam at night to suck the blood of living people -- as able to turn into bats. As a result of the pervasive influence of this novel, many people now assume that a vampire's being able to turn into a bat is an essential part of vampire myths. Statement2: However, this assumption is false, for vampire myths existed in europe long before stoker's book.
hypothesis: Premise1: At least one of the european vampire myths that predated stoker's book did not portray vampires as able to turn into bats.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: At least one of the european vampire myths that predated stoker's book did not portray vampires as strictly nocturnal.

premise: Statement1: In a study, one group of volunteers was fed a high-protein, low-carbohydrate diet ; another group was fed a low-protein, high-carbohydrate diet. Both diets contained the same number of calories, and each volunteer's diet prior to the experiment had contained moderate levels of proteins and carbohydrates. Statement2: After ten days, those on the low-carbohydrate diet had lost more weight than those on the high-carbohydrate diet. Statement3: Thus, the most effective way to lose body fat is to eat much protein and shun carbohydrates.
hypothesis: Premise1: A low-protein, high-carbohydrate diet causes the human body to retain water, the added weight of which largely compensates for the weight of any body fat lost, whereas a high-protein, low-carbohydrate diet does not.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In the experiment, the volunteers on the high- carbohydrate diet engaged in regular exercise of a kind known to produce weight loss, and those on the low-carbohydrate diet did not.

premise: Statement1: It is obvious that one ought to have a will stating how one wishes one's estate to be distributed. Statement2: This can easily be seen from the fact that, according to current laws, in the absence of a legal will distant relatives whom one has never even met have a greater legal right to one's estate than one's beloved friends do.
hypothesis: Premise1: People are generally not indifferent about how their estates are distributed.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: One's beloved friends have a greater legal right to one's estate than one's distant relatives do.

premise: Statement1: Editorial : our political discussions tend to focus largely on the flaws of our nation's leaders, but we need to remind ourselves that these leaders were chosen democratically. Statement2: The real question that needs answering is how our nation's institutions and procedures enable such people to attain positions of power. Statement3: Thus, to focus our attention on the flaws of our leaders is to indulge in a pointless distraction.
hypothesis: Premise1: Examining an individual leader's personal flaws does not reveal anything about how the nation's institutions and procedures influence the selection of leaders.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: As yet, no one in the nation has made the effort to critically examine the details of the nation's institutions and procedures.

premise: Statement1: Archaeologist : neanderthals, a human-like species living 60,000 years ago, probably preserved meat by smoking it. Statement2: Burnt lichen and grass have been found in many neanderthal fireplaces. Statement3: A fire of lichen and grass produces a lot of smoke but does not produce nearly as much heat or light as a wood fire.
hypothesis: Premise1: In the region containing the neanderthal fireplaces in which lichen and grass were burnt, no plants that could be burned more effectively to produce heat or light were available 60, 000 years ago.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In close proximity to the fireplaces with lichen and grass are other fireplaces that, evidence suggests, burned material that produced more heat than smoke.

premise: Statement1: Campaigning for election to provincial or state office frequently requires that a candidate spend much time and energy catering to the interests of national party officials who can help the candidate to win office. Statement2: The elected officials who campaign for reelection while they are in office thus often fail to serve the interests of their local consistencies.
hypothesis: Premise1: Catering to the interests of national party officials sometimes conflicts with serving the interests of a provincial or state official's local constituencies.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The interests of local constituencies are well served only by elected officials who do not cater to the interests of national party officials.

premise: Statement1: Professor : economists argue that buying lottery tickets is an unwise use of resources, because the average payoff for the tickets sold in a lottery is much lower than the cost of a ticket. Statement2: But this reasoning is faulty. Statement3: The average amount paid out on individual insurance policies is much lower than the average cost of a policy, yet nobody would argue that purchasing insurance is an unwise use of resources.
hypothesis: Premise1: The protection against loss that insurance provides is more important to one's well-being than is the possibility of a windfall gain.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Taking small financial risks can often greatly increase one's chances of obtaining much larger benefits.

premise: Statement1: Stage performances are judged to be realistic to the degree that actors reproduce on stage the behaviors generally associated by audiences with the emotional states of the characters portrayed. Statement2: Traditional actors imitate those behaviors, whereas method actors, through recollection of personal experience, actually experience the same emotions that their characters are meant to be experiencing. Statement3: Audiences will therefore judge the performances of method actors to be more realistic than the performances of traditional actors.
hypothesis: Premise1: The behavior that results when a method actor feels a certain emotion will conform to the behavior that is generally associated by audiences with that emotion.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Performances based on an actor's own experience of emotional states are more likely to affect an audience's emotions than are performances based on imitations of the behaviors generally associated with those emotional states.

premise: Statement1: The interview is an essential part of a successful hiring program because, with it, job applicants who have personalities that are unsuited to the requirements of the job will be eliminated from consideration.
hypothesis: Premise1: Interviewers can accurately identify applicants whose personalities are unsuited to the requirements of the job.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The fit of job applicants' personalities to the requirements of the job was once the most important factor in making hiring decisions.

premise: Statement1: The olex petroleum company has recently determined that it could cut its refining costs by closing its grenville refinery and consolidating all refining at its tasberg refinery. Statement2: Closing the grenville refinery, however, would mean the immediate loss of about 1,200 jobs in the grenville area. Statement3: Eventually the lives of more than 10,000 people would be seriously disrupted. premise: Statement1: Enthusiasm for the use of calculators in the learning of mathematics is misplaced. premise: Statement1: Medical research findings are customarily not made public prior to their publication in a medical journal that has had them reviewed by a panel of experts in a process called peer review. premise: Statement1: After a hepadnavirus inserts itself into a chromosome of an animal, fragments of the virus are passed on to all of that animal's descendants. hypothesis: Premise1: Many practitioners and patients neglect more effective conventional medicines in favor of herbal remedies.

premise: Statement1: In an experiment, volunteers walked individually through a dark, abandoned theater. premise: Statement1: Recently, highly skilled workers in eastern europe have left jobs in record numbers to emigrate to the west. Statement2: It is therefore likely that skilled workers who remain in eastern europe are in high demand in their home countries.

premise: Statement1: The prevailing view among historians is that medieval european peasants were deeply dedicated to religion. premise: Statement1: When volcanic lava solidifies, it becomes uniformly magnetized in the direction in which the earth's magnetic field points. premise: Statement1: Industrial scientists have hypothesized that much of the nitrous oxide that contributes to air pollution originates from the burning of organic matter in forest fires. premise: Statement1: Taken together, some 2,000 stocks recommended on a popular television show over the course of the past 12 years by the show's guests, most of whom are successful consultants for multibillion-dollar stock portfolios, performed less successfully than the market as a whole for this 12-year period. premise: Statement1: Economist : some sociologists argue that because capitalism intrinsically involves competition, it weakens the ties between the people of a community. premise: Statement1: To prevent a newly built dam on the chiff river from blocking the route of fish migrating to breeding grounds upstream, the dam includes a fish pass, a mechanism designed to allow fish through the dam. premise: Statement1: It has been claimed that television networks should provide equal time for the presentation of opposing views whenever a television program concerns scientific issues -- such as those raised by the claims of environmentalists -- about which people disagree. premise: Statement1: Tissue biopsies taken on patients who have undergone throat surgery show that those who snored frequently were significantly more likely to have serious abnormalities in their throat muscles than those who snored rarely or not at all. premise: Statement1: Copyright laws protect the rights of writers to profits earned from their writings, whereas patent laws protect inventors'rights to profits earned from their inventions. premise: Statement1: A successful coffee stand that serves the workers in a large office building plans to increase its profits by adding oatmeal-a hot cereal popular among the workers-to its menu. premise: Statement1: Rabbits were introduced to numa island in the nineteenth century. Statement2: Overgrazing by the enormous population of rabbits now menaces the island's agriculture. premise: Statement1: The stated goal of the government's funding program for the arts is to encourage the creation of works of artistic excellence. premise: Statement1: Many people suffer an allergic reaction to sulfites, including those that are commonly added to wine as preservatives. premise: Statement1: Scientists have shown that older bees, which usually forage outside the hive for food, tend to have larger brains than do younger bees, which usually do not forage but instead remain in the hive to tend to newly hatched bees. premise: Statement1: Science writer : all scientists have beliefs and values that might slant their interpretations of the data from which they draw their conclusions. premise: Statement1: Art historian : successful forgeries tend to be those most recently painted. premise: Statement1: Birds have been said to be descended from certain birdlike dinosaur species with which they share distinctive structural features. premise: Statement1: Modern flamingos derive their pink coloration from pigments stored in tiny shrimp that they filter from shallow, salty waters. The shrimp get this pigment from tiny red algae that they filter through their leg bristles. Statement2: In the jurassic period ( about 200 million years ago ), both algae and shrimp were an excellent source of food for any larger animal equipped to sieve them out of the water through an anatomical strainer. The argentine pterodactyl possessed a row of thin, bristlelike teeth through which it pumped water, straining out any tiny food particles in the process. Statement3: Thus, because it was able to filter both algae and shrimp, it is reasonable to conclude that the pterodactyl acquired a pink coloration.

premise: Statement1: The number of codfish in the north atlantic has declined substantially as the population of harp seals has increased from two million to more than three million. premise: Statement1: Until now, only injectable vaccines against influenza have been available. However, since children seldom develop serious complications from influenza, no significant public health benefit would result from widespread vaccination of children using the nasal spray.

premise: Statement1: Eight large craters run in a long straight line across a geographical region. Although some of the craters contain rocks that have undergone high-pressure shocks characteristic of meteorites slamming into earth, these shocks could also have been caused by extreme volcanic events. premise: Statement1: Carl is clearly an incompetent detective. Statement2: He has solved a smaller percentage of the cases assigned to him in the last 3 years -- only 1 out of 25 -- than any other detective on the police force.

premise: Statement1: Farmer : agricultural techniques such as crop rotation that do not use commercial products may solve agricultural problems at least as well as any technique, such as pesticide application, that does use such products. premise: Statement1: Ethicist : studies have documented the capacity of placebos to reduce pain in patients who believe that they are receiving beneficial drugs. Statement2: Some doctors say that they administer placebos because medically effective treatment reinforced by the placebo effect sometimes helps patients recover faster than good treatment alone. Statement3: But administering placebos is nonetheless ethically questionable, for even if a placebo benefits a patient, a doctor might, for example, have prescribed it just to give the patient satisfaction that something was being done.

premise: Statement1: In recent years, networks of fiber-optic cable have been replacing electrical wire for transmitting telecommunications signals. premise: Statement1: Red admiral butterflies fly in a highly irregular fashion, constantly varying their speed, wing strokes, and flight path. premise: Statement1: A survey of address changes filed with post offices and driver's license bureaus over the last ten years has established that households moving out of the city of weston outnumbered households moving into the city two to one. premise: Statement1: While the total enrollment of public elementary and secondary schools in sondland is one percent higher this academic year than last academic year, the number of teachers there increased by three percent. premise: Statement1: Ramirez : the film industry claims that pirated dvds, which are usually cheaper than legitimate dvds and become available well before a film's official dvd release date, adversely affect its bottom line. premise: Statement1: Egidio's gym has been in operation for seven years, and offers regular weekly weight training and aerobic classes, as well as occasional programs in dance and martial arts. premise: Statement1: The higher the average cholesterol intake among the residents of a nation, the higher the incidence of heart disease in that nation ; the lower the average cholesterol intake, the lower the incidence of heart disease. premise: Statement1: All oceangoing ships carry seawater ballast tanks whose weight improves stability. As a result, sea creatures often get into the tanks and are then inadvertently deposited into new habitats, where they can wreak ecological havoc. Statement3: One viable way of addressing this problem would be to empty and then immediately refill the tanks in midocean, since midocean creatures and coastal sea creatures usually can not survive in one another's habitats.

premise: Statement1: Advertisement : when your car 's engine is running at its normal operating temperature, any major brand of motor oil will protect it about as well as tuff does. premise: Statement1: Humanitarian considerations aside, sheer economics dictates that country x should institute, as country y has done, a nationwide system of air and ground transportation for conveying seriously injured persons to specialized trauma centers. premise: Statement1: Among the many temptations of the digital age, manipulation of photographs has proved particularly troublesome for science. premise: Statement1: The more demand there is for something, the easier it is to sell. premise: Statement1: Psychologists have found that candidates for top political offices who blink excessively during televised debates are judged by viewers to have done less well than competing candidates who exhibit average blink rates. premise: Statement1: Gartland has long been narrowly self-sufficient in both grain and meat.
