hypothesis: Premise1: Preferences for certain ice cream flavors are similar to preferences for candy flavors.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Because someone prefers vanilla ice cream does not mean he prefers vanilla-flavored candy.

premise: Statement1: In countries in which new life-sustaining drugs can not be patented, such drugs are sold at widely affordable prices ; those same drugs, where patented, command premium prices because the patents shield patent-holding manufacturers from competitors. Statement2: These facts show that future access to new life-sustaining drugs can be improved if the practice of granting patents on newly developed life-sustaining drugs were to be abolished everywhere.
hypothesis: Premise1: Pharmaceutical companies can afford the research that goes into the development of new drugs only if patents allow them to earn high profits.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Countries that grant patents on life-sustaining drugs almost always ban their importation from countries that do not grant such patents.

premise: Statement1: Studies suggest that, for the vast majority of people who have normal blood pressure, any amount of sodium greater than that required by the body is simply excreted and does not significantly raise blood pressure. Statement2: So only persons who have high blood pressure and whose bodies are incapable of safely processing excess sodium need to restrict their sodium intake.
hypothesis: Premise1: Excess sodium intake over time often destroys the body's ability to process excess sodium.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Every human being has a physiological need for at least some sodium.

premise: Statement1: A new law gives ownership of patents-documents providing exclusive right to make and sell an invention-to universities, not the government, when those patents result from government-sponsored university research. Statement2: Administrators at logos university plan to sell any patents they acquire to corporations in order to fund programs to improve undergraduate teaching.
hypothesis: Premise1: Government-sponsored research conducted at logos university for the most part duplicates research already completed by several profit-making corporations.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Profit-making corporations interested in developing products based on patents held by universities are likely to try to serve as exclusive sponsors of ongoing university research projects.

premise: Statement1: A newly discovered painting seems to be the work of one of two 17th-century artists, either the northern german johannes drechen or the frenchman louis birelle, who sometimes painted in the same style as drechen. Statement2: Analysis of the carved picture frame, which has been identified as the painting's original 17th century frame, showed that it is made of wood found widely in northern germany at the time, but rare in the part of france where birelle lived. Statement3: This shows that the painting is most likely the work of drechen.
hypothesis: Premise1: The frame was made from wood local to the region where the picture was painted.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Sometimes a painting so closely resembles others of its era that no expert is able to confidently decide who painted it.

premise: Statement1: In ancient greece, court witnesses were not crossexamined and the jury, selected from the citizenry, received no guidance on points of law ; thus, it was extremely important for litigants to make a good impression on the jurors. Statement2: For this reason, courtroom oratory by litigants is a good source of data on the common conceptions of morality held by the citizens of ancient greece.
hypothesis: Premise1: Litigants believed jurors were likely to be impressed by litigants whose professed moral code most resembled their own.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Litigants believed jurors were more likely to be impressed by litigants whose personality they preferred.

premise: Statement1: Workers may complain about many things at work, but stress is not high on the list. Statement2: In fact, in a recent survey a majority placed boredom at the top of their list of complaints. Statement3: The assumption that job-related stress is the most serious problem for workers in the corporate world is thus simply not warranted.
hypothesis: Premise1: Workers who complain of boredom exhibit more stress-related symptoms than do those who claim their work is interesting.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Workers are less likely to complain about work if they feel that their jobs are secure.

premise: Statement1: A certain auto repair shop services a particular make of car in the downtown region of a particular city. Statement2: Three years ago, 4 % of all repairs at this shop were not successful --- the mechanics were not able to restore the car to a drivable state. This year, only 2 % of all repairs were not successful. Statement3: Clearly, the mechanics training course, given over a year ago, has considerably increased the skill of mechanics at this particular shop, so that fewer cars are beyond their ability to repair.
hypothesis: Premise1: The total number of cars served per year by this shop has increased by 35% over the past three years.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: A new shop uptown specializes in repairing cars of this make more than six years old, and most of these older cars are taken to this shop.

premise: Statement1: Ethicist : on average, animals raised on grain must be fed sixteen pounds of grain to produce one pound of meat. Statement2: A pound of meat is more nutritious for humans than a pound of grain, but sixteen pounds of grain could feed many more people than could a pound of meat. Statement3: With grain yields leveling off, large areas of farmland going out of production each year, and the population rapidly expanding, we must accept the fact that consumption of meat will soon be morally unacceptable.
hypothesis: Premise1: Often, cattle or sheep can be raised to maturity on grass from pastureland that is unsuitable for any other kind of farming.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Nutritionists agree that a diet composed solely of grain products is not adequate for human health.

premise: Statement1: Doctors in britain have long suspected that patients who wear tinted eyeglasses are abnormally prone to depression and hypochondria. Statement2: Psychological tests given there to hospital patients admitted for physical complaints like heart pain and digestive distress confirmed such a relationship. Statement3: Perhaps people whose relationship to the world is psychologically painful choose such glasses to reduce visual stimulation, which is perceived as irritating. Statement4: At any rate, it can be concluded that when such glasses are worn, it is because the wearer has a tendency to be depressed or hypochondriacal.
hypothesis: Premise1: The tinting does not dim light to the eye enough to depress the wearer's mood substantially.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: For hypochondriacs wearing tinted glasses, the glasses serve as a visual signal to others that the wearer's health is delicate.

premise: Statement1: Newspaper editorial : many pharmaceutical companies develop" me too" drugs, drugs designed to duplicate, more or less, the effect of another company's product that is already on the market. Statement2: Some critics object that such development constitutes an unnecessary, redundant use of resources that provides no new benefits to consumers. However, the entry of" me too" drugs into the market can result in a price reduction for the drugs they resemble. Statement3: Therefore, " me too" drugs can indeed benefit consumers.
hypothesis: Premise1: Some "me too" drugs turn out to be more effective than the drugs they were designed to imitate.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: If all pharmaceutical companies developed "me too" drugs, fewer innovative drugs would be developed.

premise: Statement1: The ergonomic society conducted a study that indicated that many people develop severe back problems during adulthood, and that virtually all such people who received chiropractic treatment showed great improvement. Statement2: Therefore, in order to minimize the proportion of the population that suffers from back pain, the ergonomic society recommended that chiropractic treatment be directed toward those adults who suffer from severe back problems.
hypothesis: Premise1: Individuals who receive chiropractic or other treatment prior to developing severe back problems are not less likely to develop back pain than those who do not.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Any person who receives chiropractic treatment for back pain may also benefit from other forms of treatment.

premise: Statement1: Because a large disparity in pay between the public and private sectors has developed in recent years, many experienced and extremely capable government administrators have quit their posts and taken positions in private-sector management. Statement2: Government will be able to recapture these capable administrators by raising salaries to a level comparable to those of the private sector. Statement3: In that way, the functioning of public agencies will be improved.
hypothesis: Premise1: People who moved from jobs in government administration to private-sector management would choose to change careers again.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: If the disparity in pay between government administration and private-sector management increases, administrators will move to the public sector in large numbers.

premise: Statement1: Editorialist : some people propose that, to raise revenues and encourage conservation, our country's taxes on oil, gasoline, and coal should be increased. Statement2: Such a tax increase, however, would do more harm than good. Statement3: By raising energy costs, the tax increase would decrease our competitiveness with other countries. Statement4: Many families would be unfairly burdened with higher transportation costs. Statement5: Finally, by reducing the demand for energy, the tax increase would reduce the number of energy production jobs.
hypothesis: Premise1: The proposed tax increase would be larger for some energy sources than for others.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The editorialist's country's budget deficit will decrease if the energy tax increase is implemented, thus benefiting the economy.

premise: Statement1: A drug that is highly effective in treating many types of infection can, at present, be obtained only from the bark of the ibora, a tree that is quite rare in the wild. It takes the bark of 5,000 trees to make one kilogram of the drug. Statement2: It follows, therefore, that continued production of the drug must inevitably lead to the ibora 's extinction.
hypothesis: Premise1: The ibora can be propagated from cuttings and grown under cultivation.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The drug made from ibora bark is expensive to produce.

premise: Statement1: Geologist : a geological fault in the mountain under which the proposed nuclear waste storage facility would be buried could, after a few thousand years, cause the contents to seep out or water to seep in. Since nuclear waste remains dangerous for up to 25,000 years, such seepage would be disastrous. Statement2: So we should not place a nuclear waste storage facility under this mountain until scientists investigate whether this mountain has any geological faults.
hypothesis: Premise1: The scientists' investigation would conclusively show whether or not the mountain has any geological faults.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Nuclear waste could be stored in the proposed facility on a temporary basis.

premise: Statement1: For over two centuries, no one had been able to make damascus blades-blades with a distinctive serpentine surface pattern-but a contemporary sword maker may just have rediscovered how. Statement2: Using iron with trace impurities that precisely matched those present in the iron used in historic damascus blades, this contemporary sword maker seems to have finally hit on an intricate process by which he can produce a blade indistinguishable from a true damascus blade.
hypothesis: Premise1: Production of damascus blades by sword makers of the past ceased abruptly after those sword makers' original source of iron became exhausted.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The iron with which the contemporary sword maker made damascus blades came from a source of iron that was unknown two centuries ago.

premise: Statement1: There are two supposedly conflicting hypotheses as to what makes for great national leaders : one is that such leaders successfully shape public opinion, and the other is that they are adept at reacting to it. However, treating these hypotheses as mutually exclusive is evidently a mistake. Statement2: All leaders who have had success getting their programs passed by their country's legislature have been adroit both in shaping and reacting to public opinion.
hypothesis: Premise1: Having success getting programs passed by the legislature is indicative of being a great national leader.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: It is impossible to successfully shape public opinion without in some way reacting to it.

premise: Statement1: Editor : sent2: many candidates say that if elected they will reduce governmental intrusion into voters'lives. But voters actually elect politicians who instead promise that the government will provide assistance to solve their most pressing problems. Statement2: Governmental assistance, however, costs money, and money can come only from taxes, which can be considered a form of governmental intrusion. Statement3: Thus, governmental intrusion into the lives of voters will rarely be substantially reduced over time in a democracy.
hypothesis: Premise1: Politicians who win their elections usually keep their campaign promises.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Politicians never promise what they really intend to do once in office.

premise: Statement1: Economist : technology now changes so rapidly that workers need periodic retraining. Such retraining can be efficient only if it allows individual companies to meet their own shortterm needs. Statement2: Hence, large governmental job retraining programs are no longer a viable option in the effort to retrain workers efficiently.
hypothesis: Premise1: Large governmental job retraining programs do not meet the short-term needs of individual companies.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: No single type of retraining program is most efficient at retraining technological workers.

premise: Statement1: Researchers hope to find clues about the a'mk peoples who lived in the kaumpta region about one thousand years ago but who left few obvious traces. Statement2: The researchers plan to hire the few remaining shamans of the modern-day indigenous people in kaumpta, who are believed to be descended from the a'mk, to lead them to ancestral sites that may be the remains of a'mk buildings or ceremonial spaces. Statement3: The shamans were taught the location of such sites as part of their traditional training as youths, and their knowledge of traditional kaumpta customs may help determine the nature of any sites the researchers find.
hypothesis: Premise1: The shamans have a reasonably accurate memory of a'mk sites they learned about as youths.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most or all a'mk sites are likely to be found within the kaumpta region.

premise: Statement1: Many important types of medicine have been developed from substances discovered in plants that grow only in tropical rain forests. Statement2: There are thousands of plant species in these rain forests that have not yet been studied by scientists, and it is very likely that many such plants also contain substances of medicinal value. Statement3: Thus, if the tropical rain forests are not preserved, important types of medicine will never be developed.
hypothesis: Premise1: There are substances of medicinal value contained in tropical rain forest plants not yet studied by scientists that differ from those substances already discovered in tropical rain forest plants.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Any substance of medicinal value contained in plant species indigenous to tropical rain forests will eventually be discovered if those species are studied by scientists.

premise: Statement1: Over the last five years, every new major alternative-energy initiative that initially was promised government funding has since seen that funding severely curtailed. In no such case has the government come even close to providing the level of funds initially earmarked for these projects. Statement2: Since large corporations have made it a point to discourage alternative-energy projects, it is likely that the corporations'actions influenced the government's funding decisions.
hypothesis: Premise1: The only research projects whose government funding has been severely curtailed are those that large corporations have made it a point to discourage.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The funding initially earmarked for a government project is always subject to change, given the mechanisms by which the political process operates.

premise: Statement1: Reducing stress lessens a person's sensitivity to pain. Statement2: This is the conclusion reached by researchers who played extended audiotapes to patients before they underwent surgery and afterward while they were recovering. Statement3: One tape consisted of conversation ; the other consisted of music. Statement4: Those who listened only to the latter tape required less anesthesia during surgery and fewer painkillers afterward than those who listened only to the former tape.
hypothesis: Premise1: Listening to music reduces stress.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Both anesthesia and painkillers tend to reduce stress.

premise: Statement1: There are many structural and thematic similarities between piers plowman by langland ( 1330-1400 ) and house of fame by chaucer ( 1342-1400 ), two middle english poems relating dream visions. Statement2: Some critics have argued that because a number of the shared elements are uncommon in middle english poetry, and because langland's poem probably predates chaucer's by a few years, chaucer was most likely influenced by piers plowman when writing house of fame.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many of the themes and structures of piers plowman are also found in latin, italian, french works with which chaucer could well have been familiar.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: House of fame survives in only three manuscript copies, substantially fewer than the number of manuscript copies that exist of piers plowman.

premise: Statement1: Each of two drugs, s and t, greatly reduces the effects of potentially fatal heart attacks if given as soon as possible after the attack begins, but a trial has shown that use of drug t instead of drug s would prevent death in one additional case out of 120. sent2: drug t, however, costs $ 2,000 more per treatment than drug s. sent3: therefore society is presented with a stark policy decision : whether or not to pay the $ 240,000 it would cost to use drug t in order to save one additional patient.
hypothesis: Premise1: There is no quick, practical, and relatively inexpensive way of telling for any individual case whether drug s will be as effective as drug t.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: After a heart attack, drug t remains relatively effective if given at a time at which drug s is no longer effective.

premise: Statement1: Medical researcher : scientists compared a large group of joggers who habitually stretch before jogging to an equal number of joggers who do not stretch before jogging. Statement2: Both groups of joggers incurred roughly the same number of injuries. Statement3: This indicates that stretching before jogging does not help to prevent injuries.
hypothesis: Premise1: The more prone a jogger is to jogging injuries, the more likely he or she is to develop the habit of performing stretches before jogging.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most jogging injuries result from falls, collisions, and other mishaps on which the flexibility resulting from stretching would have little if any effect.

premise: Statement1: Medical researcher : as expected, records covering the last four years of ten major hospitals indicate that babies born prematurely were more likely to have low birth weights and to suffer from health problems than were babies not born prematurely. Statement2: These records also indicate that mothers who had received adequate prenatal care were less likely to have low birth weight babies than were mothers who had received inadequate prenatal care. Statement3: Adequate prenatal care, therefore, significantly decreases the risk of low birth weight babies.
hypothesis: Premise1: Mothers giving birth prematurely are routinely classified by hospitals as having received inadequate prenatal care when the record of that care is not available.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The hospital records indicate that many babies that are born with normal birth weights are born to mothers who had inadequate prenatal care.

premise: Statement1: Magazine article : sugar consumption may exacerbate attention deficit disorder ( add ) in children. Statement2: A recent study found that children produce large amounts of adrenaline within hours after consuming large amounts of sugar. This increase in adrenaline is especially noticeable if the source of sugar is candy, in which case the sugar's effects are not ameliorated by the ingestion of other foodstuffs.
hypothesis: Premise1: Increased adrenaline production can make add more severe in children.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Sugar consumed with food substances other than candy does not substantially increase the level of adrenaline in the bloodstream of children with add.

premise: Statement1: Traditional hatcheries raise fish in featureless environments and subject them to dull routines, whereas new, experimental hatcheries raise fish in visually stimulating environments with varied routines. When released into the wild, fish from the experimental hatcheries are bolder than those from traditional hatcheries in exploring new environments and trying new types of food. Statement2: Fish raised in the experimental hatcheries, therefore, are more likely to survive after their release.
hypothesis: Premise1: Some fish raised in traditional hatcheries die because they are too timid in their foraging for food.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Fish in the wild always live in visually stimulating environments.

premise: Statement1: People with higher-than-average blood levels of a normal dietary by-product called homocysteine are twice as likely to be diagnosed with alzheimer's disease as are those with average or below-average homocysteine levels. Statement2: Thus, it is likely that the risk of developing alzheimer's disease could be reduced by including in one's diet large amounts of b vitamins and folic acid, which convert homocysteine into substances known to have no relation to alzheimer's disease.
hypothesis: Premise1: Alzheimer's disease tends to increase the levels of homocysteine in the blood.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: B vitamins and folic acid are not metabolized by the body very efficiently when taken in the form of vitamin-mineral supplements.

premise: Statement1: In a transportation company, a certain syndrome often attributed to stress by medical experts afflicts a significantly higher percentage of workers in department f than in any other department. Statement2: We can conclude, therefore, that the work done in department f subjects workers to higher stress levels than does the work in the other departments in the company.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many workers who transfer into department f from elsewhere in the company soon begin to develop the syndrome.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Some experts believe that the syndrome can be caused by various factors, only one of which is high stress.

premise: Statement1: In mountainous regions, the timberline is the highest altitude at which trees grow. Statement2: In the rocky mountains, the current timberline is at the altitude above which growing season temperatures remain cooler than 10 degrees centigrade. Statement3: Fossilized remains of trees that grew 10,000 years ago have been found 100 meters above the current rocky mountain timberline. Statement4: Clearly, therefore, the climate of the rocky mountains is cooler now than it was 10,000 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: The fossils are not of species of trees that were able to tolerate cooler growing temperatures than are the species that currently grow near the timberline.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In the past 10, 000 years, the only trees to have grown above today's timberline are the trees whose fossilized remains been found.

premise: Statement1: The population of peregrine falcons declined rapidly during the 1950's and 1960's and reached an all-time low in the early 1970's. Statement2: The decline was attributed by scientists to the widespread use of the pesticide ddt in rural areas.
hypothesis: Premise1: In the time since the use of ddt was banned in 1972, the population of peregrine falcons has been steadily increasing.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Peregrine falcons, like other birds of prey, abandon eggs that have fallen out of the nest, even if the eggs remain intact.

premise: Statement1: The reason j.s. Bach is remembered is not that he had a high ratio of outstanding compositions to mediocre compositions. It is rather because he was such a prolific composer. Statement2: He wrote more than a thousand full-fledged compositions, so it was inevitable that some of them would be outstanding and, being outstanding, survive the ages.
hypothesis: Premise1: Several of bach's contemporaries who produced more works than he did have been largely forgotten.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: There are a few highly regarded composers who wrote a comparatively small number of compositions.

premise: Statement1: Most small children are flat-footed. Statement2: This failure of the foot to assume its natural arch, if it persists past early childhood can sometimes result in discomfort and even pain later in life. Statement3: Traditionally, flat-footedness in children has been treated by having the children wear special shoes that give extra support to the foot, in order to foster the development of the arch.
hypothesis: Premise1: Flat-footed children who do not wear the special shoes are as likely to develop natural arches as are flat-footed children who wear the special shoes.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Although most children's flat-footedness is corrected by the time the children reach puberty, some people remain flat-footed for life.

premise: Statement1: A survey of entrepreneurs who started companies last year shows that while virtually all did substantial preparatory research and planning, only half used that work to produce a formal business plan. Statement2: Since, on average, the entrepreneurs without formal plans secured the capital they needed in half the time of those with plans, these survey results indicate that, in general, formal plans did not help the entrepreneurs who produced them to secure the capital they needed.
hypothesis: Premise1: Among the entrepreneurs surveyed, those who did not produce formal business plans sought and received a much larger proportion of their capital from investors with whom they had a long-standing business relationship.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Companies started by entrepreneurs who had used formal business plans to attract investment were on the whole as profitable in their first year as were companies started by entrepreneurs who had not produced such plans.

premise: Statement1: On the caribbean island of guadeloupe, a researcher examined 35 patients with atypical parkinson's disease and compared their eating habits to those of 65 healthy adults. Statement2: She found that all of the patients with atypical parkinson's regularly ate the tropical fruits soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle, whereas only 10 of the healthy adults regularly ate these fruits. Statement3: From this, she concluded that eating these fruits causes atypical parkinson's.
hypothesis: Premise1: For many of the atypical parkinson's patients, their symptoms stopped getting worse, and in some cases actually abated, when they stopped eating soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The 10 healthy adults who regularly ate soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle ate significantly greater quantities of these fruits, on average, than did the 35 atypical parkinson's patients.

premise: Statement1: In the nation of partoria, large trucks currently have a much higher rate of traffic accidents per mile driven than other vehicles do. Statement2: However, the very largest trucks-those with three trailers-had less than a third of the accident rate of single- and double-trailer trucks. Statement3: Clearly, therefore, one way for partoria to reduce the number of traffic accidents would be to require shippers to increase their use of triple-trailer trucks.
hypothesis: Premise1: Partorian trucking companies currently use triple-trailer trucks only for long trips using major highways, which is the safest kind of trip for large trucks.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: No matter what changes partoria makes in the regulation of trucking, it will have to keep some smaller roads off-limits to all large trucks.

premise: Statement1: Gilbert : this food label is mistaken. It says that these cookies contain only natural ingredients, but they contain alphahydroxy acids that are chemically synthesized by the cookie company at their plant. Statement2: Sabina : the label is not mistaken. Statement3: After all, alphahydroxy acids also are found occurring naturally in sugarcane.
hypothesis: Premise1: All substances except those that do not occur naturally in any source are considered natural.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Not all chemicals that are part of the manufacturing process are ingredients of the cookies.

premise: Statement1: Feathers recently taken from seabirds stuffed and preserved in the 1880s have been found to contain only half as much mercury as feathers recently taken from living birds of the same species. Statement2: Since mercury that accumulates in a seabird's feathers as the feathers grow is derived from fish eaten by the bird, these results indicate that mercury levels in saltwater fish are higher now than they were 100 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: The process used to preserve birds in the 1880s did not substantially decrease the amount of mercury in the birds' feathers
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The amount of mercury in a saltwater fish depends on the amount of pollution in the ocean habitat of the fish

premise: Statement1: Caffeine can kill or inhibit the growth of the larvae of several species of insects. Statement2: One recent experiment showed that tobacco hornworm larvae die when they ingest a preparation that consists, in part, of finely powdered tea leaves, which contain caffeine. Statement3: This result is evidence for the hypothesis that the presence of non-negligible quantities of caffeine in various parts of many diverse species of plants is not accidental but evolved as a defense for those plants.
hypothesis: Premise1: Caffeine-producing plants or their ancestors have at some time been subject to being fed upon by creatures sensitive to caffeine
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The tobacco plant is among the plant species that produce caffeine for their own defense

premise: Statement1: Political advertisement : mayor delmont's critics complain about the jobs that were lost in the city under delmont's leadership. Yet the fact is that not only were more jobs created than were eliminated, but the average pay for these new jobs has been higher than the average pay for jobs citywide every year since delmont took office. Statement2: So there can be no question that throughout delmont's tenure the average paycheck in this city has been getting steadily bigger.
hypothesis: Premise1: The average pay for jobs eliminated in the city during mayor delmont's tenure has been roughly equal every year to the average pay for jobs citywide.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Average pay in the city was at a ten-year low when mayor delmont took office.

premise: Statement1: Government official : currently many business travelers rely on a one-hour flight between the cities, but complain about congestion and delays at the airport. Given this option, many who fly between the two cities during the week will choose the railway, since it will allow them to travel between the city centers in less time than a flight with the expected delays plus the commute to and from airports would take. Statement2: The new high-speed railway between hamport and dudley, aplandia 's two major financial hubs, will be able to make the same trip in slightly less than three hours.
hypothesis: Premise1: The proposed speed at which trains are to travel will produce static electricity that will interfere with other trains' equipment, thereby causing significant delays.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Twice within the last two decades there have been significant recessions in aplandia.

premise: Statement1: If the government increases its funding for civilian scientific research, private patrons and industries will believe that such research has become primarily the government's responsibility. Statement2: When they believe that research is no longer primarily their responsibility, private patrons and industries will decrease their contributions toward research. Statement3: Therefore, in order to keep from depressing the overall level of funding for civilian scientific research, the government should not increase its own funding.
hypothesis: Premise1: Any increase in government funding would displace more private funding for funding for civilian scientific research than it would provide.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Funding for civilian scientific research is currently at the highest possible level.

premise: Statement1: Chronic fatigue syndrome is characterized by prolonged fatigue, muscular pain, and neurological problems. Statement2: It is not known whether these symptoms are all caused by a single virus or whether each symptom is the result of a separate viral infection. Statement3: A newly synthesized drug has been tested on those who suffer from chronic fatigue syndrome. Although the specific antiviral effects of this drug are unknown, it has lessened the severity of all of the symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome. Statement4: Thus there is evidence that chronic fatigue syndrome is, in fact, caused by one virus.
hypothesis: Premise1: It is more likely that the new drug counteracts one virus than that it counteracts several viruses.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: All those who suffer from prolonged fatigue also suffer from neurological problems.

premise: Statement1: An overwhelming proportion of the most productive employees at saleco's regional offices work not eight hours a day, five days a week, as do other saleco employees, but rather ten hours a day, four days a week, with friday off. Statement2: Noting this phenomenon, saleco's president plans to increase overall productivity by keeping the offices closed on fridays and having all employees work the same schedule-ten hours a day, four days a week.
hypothesis: Premise1: Working ten hours a day has allowed the most productive employees to work two hours alone each day in their respective offices relatively undisturbed by fellow employees.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Typically, a saleco employee's least productive hours in the workplace are the early afternoon hours.

premise: Statement1: Lawmaker : raising taxes is not the only means of reducing government debt. The government's stockpile of helium is worth 25 percent more, at current market prices, than the debt accumulated in acquiring and storing it. Statement2: Therefore, by selling the helium, the government can not only pay off that debt but reduce its overall debt as well.
hypothesis: Premise1: Attempts to sell the government's helium will not depress the market price of helium by more than 25 percent.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Twenty-five percent of the debt the government has accumulated in stockpiling helium is not an insignificant portion of the government's total debt.

premise: Statement1: Automobile emissions are a significant source of air pollutants, and cars over five years old typically generate significantly greater amounts of pollutants than newer cars. In torinia, which has recently built its first automobile manufacturing plant, most cars are over five years old. Statement2: Aiming to boost torinia's economy and reduce air pollution, the government plans to introduce incentives for torinians to scrap their old cars every five years and replace them with new ones.
hypothesis: Premise1: The manufacture and the scrapping of cars each generate significant amounts of air pollutants.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The largest source of atmospheric pollutants in torinia is not automobile emissions, but emissions from power plants.

Test subjects who are chronically deprived of rem sleep become irritable during waking life. Statement3: This shows that rem sleep relieves the stresses of waking life.

premise: Statement1: Earthworms, vital to the health of soil, prefer soil that is approximately neutral on the acid-to-alkaline scale. premise: Statement1: Researcher : people are able to tell whether a person is extroverted just by looking at pictures in which the person has a neutral expression. Statement2: Since people are also able to tell whether a chimpanzee behaves dominantly just by looking at a picture of the chimpanzee's expressionless face, and since both humans and chimpanzees are primates, we conclude that this ability is probably not acquired solely through culture but rather as a result of primate biology.
hypothesis: Premise1: Extroversion in people and dominant behavior in chimpanzees are both indicators of a genetic predisposition to assertiveness.

premise: Statement1: Businessman : sales are the most important part of any business. Statement2: Marketing and communications are never as effective as a strong sales team. Statement4: Our company should eliminate every department except the sales team.
hypothesis: Premise1: The sales team's effectiveness depends on contributions from the other departments.

premise: Statement1: Although there is no record of poet edmund spenser 's parentage, we do know that as a youth spenser attended the merchant tailors'school in london for a period between 1560 and 1570. records from this time indicate that the merchant tailors'guild then had only three members named spenser : robert spenser, listed as a gentleman ; nicholas spenser, elected the guild 's warden in 1568 ; and john spenser, listed as a" journeyman cloth-maker. " Statement2: Of these, the last was likely the least affluent of the three-and most likely edmund 's father, since school accounting records list edmund as a scholar who attended the school at a reduced fee.

premise: Statement1: The legislature of the philippines voted recently to abolish the death penalty. In contrast, the death penalty remains legal in the united states. premise: Statement1: Robin : when a region's economy is faltering, many people lose their jobs. Statement2: As a result, spending on consumer goods declines, leading in turn to more lost jobs and a worsening of the economy. Statement3: Eventually, the economy becomes so bad that prices collapse ; the lower prices encourage people to increase spending on consumer goods, and this higher spending results in economic improvement. hypothesis: Premise1: Even people who do not lose their jobs spend less in bad economic times and thus have savings available to spend when prices collapse.

premise: Statement1: Opponents of peat harvesting in this country argue that it would alter the ecological balance of our peatrich wetlands and that, as a direct consequence of this, much of the country's water supply would be threatened with contamination. But this can not be true, for in ireland, where peat has been harvested for centuries, the water supply is not contaminated. Statement2: We can safely proceed with the harvesting of peat.
hypothesis: Premise1: The original ecology of the peat-harvesting areas of ireland was virtually identical to that of the undisturbed wetlands of this country.

premise: Statement1: Older united states automobiles have been identified as contributing disproportionately to global air pollution. The requirement in many jurisdictions that automobiles pass emission-control inspections has had the effect of taking many such automobiles out of service in the united states, as they fail inspection and their owners opt to buy newer automobiles. Statement2: Thus the burden of pollution such older united states automobiles contribute to the global atmosphere will be gradually reduced over the next decade.
hypothesis: Premise1: There is a thriving market for used older united states automobiles that are exported to regions that have no emission-control regulations.

premise: Statement1: Builder : within ten years, most of the new homes constructed in north america will have steel frameworks rather than wood ones. Statement2: After all, two-by-fours and two-by-sixes -- the sizes of lumber most commonly used in home construction -- are deteriorating in quality and increasing in cost, while environment-friendly steel is decreasing in cost. In addition, unlike wood, steel will not warp, rot, or split.
hypothesis: Premise1: Training home construction workers to work with steel is very costly.

premise: Statement1: People who have political power tend to see new technologies as a means of extending or protecting their power, whereas they generally see new ethical arguments and ideas as a threat to it. Statement2: Therefore, technical ingenuity usually brings benefits to those who have this ingenuity, whereas ethical inventiveness brings only pain to those who have this inventiveness.
hypothesis: Premise1: Politically powerful people tend to reward those who they believe are useful to them and to punish those who they believe are a threat.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: New technologies are often used by people who strive to defeat those who currently have political power.

premise: Statement1: When amphibians first appeared on earth millions of years ago, the amount of ultraviolet radiation penetrating earth's atmosphere was much greater than it is today. Statement2: Therefore, current dramatic decreases in amphibian populations can not be the result of recent increases in ultraviolet radiation penetrating earth's atmosphere.
hypothesis: Premise1: The eggs of modern amphibians are not significantly more vulnerable to ultraviolet radiation than the eggs of the first amphibians were.

premise: Statement1: Psychiatrist : in treating first-year students at this university, i have noticed that those reporting the highest levels of spending on recreation score at about the same level on standard screening instruments for anxiety and depression as those reporting the lowest levels of spending on recreation. Statement2: This suggests that the first-year students with high levels of spending on recreation could reduce that spending without increasing their anxiety or depression.
hypothesis: Premise1: Among adults between the ages of 40 and 60, increased levels of spending on recreation are strongly correlated with decreased levels of anxiety and depression.

premise: Statement1: Scientist : there is a lot of concern that human behavior may be responsible for large-scale climate change. premise: Statement1: More and more computer programs that provide solutions to mathematical problems in engineering are being produced, and it is thus increasingly unnecessary for practicing engineers to have a thorough understanding of fundamental mathematical principles. premise: Statement1: Chef : this mussel recipe's first step is to sprinkle the live mussels with cornmeal. premise: Statement1: Drama critic : audiences will enjoy warner's latest play, about the disintegration of a family, because of the superb acting. premise: Statement1: In a study of the effect of radiation from nuclear weapons plants on people living in areas near them, researchers compared death rates in the areas near the plants with death rates in areas that had no such plants. hypothesis: Premise1: Exposure to nuclear radiation can cause many serious diseases that do not necessarily result in death.

premise: Statement1: When a certain gland becomes cancerous in humans, it produces high levels of a particular protein. premise: Statement1: In north america there has been an explosion of public interest in, and enjoyment of, opera over the last three decades. premise: Statement1: When a society undergoes slow change, its younger members find great value in the advice of its older members. But when a society undergoes rapid change, young people think that little in the experience of their elders is relevant to them, and so do not value their advice. Statement2: Thus, we may measure the rate at which a society is changing by measuring the amount of deference its younger members show to their elders.

premise: Statement1: In 1955, legislation in a certain country gave the government increased control over industrial workplace safety conditions. Among the high-risk industries in that country, the likelihood that a worker will suffer a serious injury has decreased since 1955. sent3: the legislation, therefore, has increased overall worker safety within high-risk industries.

premise: Statement1: The indonesian mimic octopus is able to camouflage itself from predators by blending into the background. Sometimes, though, even when a predator is nearby, the mimic octopus takes on a color pattern that is not present in the immediate environment. premise: Statement1: Journalist : the advice of social scientists is frequently overlooked by politicians making social policy. Statement2: Because it is not unreasonable to discount scientific assertions backed by weak evidence, politicians should not generally be criticized for ignoring social science, for social scientists, unlike physical scientists, seldom agree on the claims made even within their own specialty.

premise: Statement1: Thirty years ago, the percentage of the british people who vacationed in foreign countries was very small compared with the large percentage of the british population who travel abroad for vacations now. premise: Statement1: Young people believe efforts to reduce pollution, poverty, and war are doomed to failure. premise: Statement1: Nutritionist : recently a craze has developed for home juicers, $ 300 machines that separate the pulp of fruits and vegetables from the juice they contain. premise: Statement1: The city of workney, in raising bus fares from $ 1.00 to $ 1.25, proposed that 18 fare tokens be sold for $ 20.00 to alleviate the extra burden of the fare increase on the city's low-income residents. premise: Statement1: Millions of irreplaceable exhibits in natural history museums are currently allowed to decay. premise: Statement1: Analyst : the increasing complexity of computers may lead those who pursue a career in computer programming to think that job security and higher wages can be attained by becoming more specialized as the field becomes more complex. premise: Statement1: Parent : the city education department is unable to distinguish between annoyances and important problems. premise: Statement1: For similar cars and drivers, automobile insurance for collision damage has always cost more in greatport than in fairmont. premise: Statement1: Researchers examined 100 people suffering from herniated disks in their backs. Five of them were found to have a defect in a particular gene. The researchers also examined 100 people who had no problems with the disks in their backs ; none had the genetic defect. premise: Statement1: Biologists studying plant life in the prehistoric rainforest by utilizing samples of ancient ice and fossilized evidence of biological processes found that most of the plants removed an astonishingly small amount of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere. premise: Statement1: Lobsters and other crustaceans eaten by humans are more likely to contract gill diseases when sewage contaminates their water. premise: Statement1: Twenty years ago the republic of rosinia produced nearly 100 million tons of potatoes, but last year the harvest barely reached 60 million tons. premise: Statement1: People who have specialized knowledge about a scientific or technical issue are systematically excluded from juries for trials where that issue is relevant. Statement2: Thus, trial by jury is not a fair means of settling disputes involving such issues.

premise: Statement1: Mayor pollister always repays his contributors by passing the laws they desire. Statement2: Since mayor pollister recently passed the city's first clean air act, he must have had a contributor to repay.

premise: Statement1: It is the mark of a superior conductor that he or she has the authority to insist, even with a top orchestra, that rehearsal work must be intensified. premise: Statement1: Outsourcing is the practice of obtaining from an independent supplier a product or service that a company has previously provided for itself. Statement2: Since a company 's chief objective is to realize the highest possible year-end profits, any product or service that can be obtained from an independent supplier for less than it would cost the company to provide the product or service on its own should be outsourced.

premise: Statement1: In 1960's studies of rats, scientists found that crowding increases the number of attacks among the animals significantly. Statement2: But in recent experiments in which rhesus monkeys were placed in crowded conditions, although there was an increase in instances of" coping" behavior-such as submissive gestures and avoidance of dominant individuals-attacks did not become any more frequent. premise: Statement1: Psychologist : identical twins are virtually the same genetically. premise: Statement1: In response to viral infection, the immune systems of mice typically produce antibodies that destroy the virus by binding to proteins on its surface. Statement2: Mice infected with the herpesvirus generally develop keratitis, a degenerative disease affecting part of the eye. Statement3: Since proteins on the surface of cells in this part of the eye closely resemble those on the herpesvirus surface, scientists hypothesize that these cases of keratitis are caused by antibodies to the herpesvirus.

premise: Statement1: Automobile dealer's advertisement : the highway traffic safety institute reports that the pz 1000 has the fewest injuries per accident of any car in its class. Statement2: This shows that the pz 1000 is one of the safest cars available today.

premise: Statement1: A student has taken twelve courses and received a b in a majority of them. Statement2: The student is now taking another course and will probably, given her record, receive a b in it.

premise: Statement1: Building a space station, in which astronauts would live for a considerable time, is essential even if the space station project were to contribute no new knowledge about space or earth that could not otherwise be obtained. Statement2: For future missions to explore mars, we will need the medical knowledge that the space station project will give us about the limits of human capacities to live in spacecraft for an extended time.

premise: Statement1: Curator : since ancient times, the fine arts were developed and sustained with the aid of large subsidies from the aristocracies and religious institutions that were the public sectors of their day ; it is doubtful that the arts would have survived without these subsidies. Statement2: Clearly, contemporary societies should fulfill their obligation as stewards of cultural heritage without the assistance of aristocracies or religious institutions, so governments must help finance the maintenance, advancement, and enrichment of the fine arts today.
hypothesis: Premise1: Serving as stewards of cultural heritage requires that contemporary societies help to maintain the fine arts.

premise: Statement1: The supernova event of 1987 is interesting in that there is still no evidence of the neutron star that current theory says should have remained after a supernova of that size. This is in spite of the fact that many of the most sensitive instruments ever developed have searched for the tell-tale pulse of radiation that neutron stars emit. Statement2: Thus, current theory is wrong in claiming that supernovas of a certain size always produce neutron stars.
hypothesis: Premise1: Sensitive astronomical instruments have detected neutron stars much farther away than the location of the 1987 supernova.

premise: Statement1: Once children begin to read they acquire new vocabulary most naturally as the indirect result of reading difficult, challenging material. Statement2: The major alternative -- direct studying of new vocabulary items -- is less natural ; most people are not so inclined. From age six to age thirteen, children learn thousands of new words a year, mostly through reading ; direct studying accounts for less than one-tenth of all new words learned. Statement3: So it is bound to be inefficient for young students to learn new vocabulary by direct studying.

premise: Statement1: Even the earliest known species of land animals, known from fossils dating from the late silurian period, 400 million years ago, show highly evolved adaptations to life on land. premise: Statement1: Fossilized teeth of an extinct species of herbivorous great ape have on them phytoliths, which are microscopic petrified remains of plants. Statement2: Since only phytoliths from certain species of plants are found on the teeth, the apes'diet must have consisted only of those plants.
hypothesis: Premise1: Plants of every type eaten by the apes left phytoliths on their teeth.

premise: Statement1: Two lakes in the pawpaw mountains, quapaw and highwater, were suffering from serious declines in their fish populations ten years ago. hypothesis: Premise1: Prior to the ban, there was practically no fishing at quapaw lake.

premise: Statement1: Crops can be traded on the futures market before they are harvested. premise: Statement1: To reduce waste of raw materials, the government of sperland is considering requiring household appliances to be broken down for salvage when discarded. premise: Statement1: Anthropologist : it was formerly believed that prehistoric homo sapiens ancestors of contemporary humans interbred with neanderthals, but dna testing of a neanderthal's remains indicates that this is not the case. premise: Statement1: Detective : laser-printer drums are easily damaged, and any nick in a drum will produce a blemish of similar dimensions on each page produced by that printer. premise: Statement1: For the past 13 years, high school guidance counselors nationwide have implemented an aggressive program to convince high school students to select careers requiring college degrees. premise: Statement1: Due to the profitable but unlawful practice of selling elephant tusks, elephants have been hunted to near extinction by unlicensed poachers. premise: Statement1: The goblin fern, which requires a thick layer of leaf litter on the forest floor, is disappearing from north american forests. premise: Statement1: Measurements of the motion of the planet uranus seem to show uranus being tugged by a force pulling it away from the sun and the inner planets. premise: Statement1: Critic : historians purport to discover the patterns inherent in the course of events. But historians actually impose, rather than find, such patterns by choosing what to include in and exclude from their historical narratives. Statement2: Thus, properly understood, histories reveal more about the presuppositions underlying different historians'attempts to understand what happened than about what actually happened.
hypothesis: Premise1: Which pattern a historian imposes upon events is affected by that historian's presuppositions.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Historians have many presuppositions in common with one another.

premise: Statement1: Scientist : laboratory animals have access to ample food, and they get relatively little exercise. Statement2: These factors can skew the results of research using animals, since such studies often rely on the assumption that the animal subjects are healthy. Statement3: For instance, animal studies that purport to show that extreme caloric restriction can extend lifespans take for granted that their subjects were not overfed to begin with.
hypothesis: Premise1: Laboratory conditions that provide animals with ample food but relatively little exercise can be unhealthy for the animals.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Laboratory animals are healthy if they are fed a carefully restricted diet and get plenty of exercise.

premise: Statement1: Cotton grass, which grows only in arctic regions, has been the only summertime source of protein available to caribou. Statement2: Caribou that do not get adequate amounts of protein in the summer are unable to reproduce the following year. premise: Statement1: A six-month public health campaign sought to limit the spread of influenza by encouraging people to take precautions such as washing their hands frequently and avoiding public places when they experience influenza symptoms. premise: Statement1: The first publicly available fantasy football league was launched in 1997, and within three years, every major football media website had launched their own sites. premise: Statement1: Archaeologists in michigan have excavated a native american camp near dumaw creek. premise: Statement1: Doctor : medication to reduce blood pressure often has unhealthy side effects. premise: Statement1: A certain experimental fungicide causes no harm to garden plants, though only if it is diluted at least to ten parts water to one part fungicide. premise: Statement1: The ability of mammals to control their internal body temperatures is a factor in the development of their brains and intelligence. premise: Statement1: An economist has argued that consumers often benefit when government permits a corporation to obtain a monopoly. premise: Statement1: In preparation for a large-scale tree-planting project, thomasville has recorded the location, species, and condition of every tree in its downtown area. premise: Statement1: Limited research indicates that therapeutic intervention before the onset of mental disorders can mitigate factors identified as major contributors to them. premise: Statement1: Grammarians have for years condemned as ungrammatical the english phrase" between you and i" , insisting that the correct phrasing is" between you and me, " with the objective case after a preposition. premise: Statement1: In contemplating major purchases, businesses often consider only whether there is enough money left from monthly revenues after paying monthly expenses to cover the cost of the purchase. premise: Statement1: In 1963, a young macaque monkey was observed venturing into a hot spring to retrieve food which had fallen in. premise: Statement1: A company that manufactures plastic products from recyclable plastic is, surprisingly, unconcerned that economic conditions may worsen, despite analysts'belief that consumers would then consider ecofriendly plastic products an expensive luxury. premise: Statement1: The effort involved in lying produces measurable physiological reactions such as a speedup of the heartbeat. premise: Statement1: Prolonged exposure to sulfur fumes permanently damages one's sense of smell. premise: Statement1: An article claims that many medical patients have an instinctual ability to predict sudden changes in their medical status. premise: Statement1: Incumbent politicians tend to win elections in times of economic prosperity and lose during recessions. premise: Statement1: Before 1986 physicists believed they could describe the universe in terms of four universal forces. hypothesis: Premise1: No previously established scientific results are incompatible with the notion of a fifth universal force.

premise: Statement1: Chemist : the molecules of a certain weed-killer are always present in two forms, one the mirror image of the other. Statement2: One form of the molecule kills weeds, while the other has no effect on them. As a result, the effectiveness of the weed-killer in a given situation is heavily influenced by which of the two forms is more concentrated in the soil, which in turn varies widely because local soil conditions will usually favor the breakdown of one form or the other. Statement3: Thus, much of the data on the effects of this weed-killer are probably misleading.
hypothesis: Premise1: Almost all of the data on the effects of the weed-killer are drawn from laboratory studies in which both forms of the weed-killer's molecules are equally concentrated in the soil and equally likely to break down in that soil.

premise: Statement1: Budget constraints have made police officials consider reassigning a considerable number of officers from traffic enforcement to work on higher-priority, serious crimes. premise: Statement1: Today's farmers plant only a handful of different strains of a given crop. Statement2: Crops lack the diversity that they had only a few generations ago. hypothesis: Premise1: Affected crops can quickly be replaced from seed banks that store many strains of those crops.

premise: Statement1: Standard archaeological techniques make it possible to determine the age of anything containing vegetable matter, but only if the object is free of minerals containing carbon. premise: Statement1: Detective : because the embezzler must have had specialized knowledge and access to internal financial records, we can presume that the embezzler worked for xyz corporation as either an accountant or an actuary. premise: Statement1: When the ancient fossils of a primitive land mammal were unearthed in new zealand, they provided the first concrete evidence that the island country had once had indigenous land mammals.
