premise: Statement1: Researcher : any country can determine which type of public school system will work best for it by investigating the public school systems of other countries. Statement2: Nationwide tests could be given in each country and other countries could adopt the system of the country that has the best scores on these tests.
hypothesis: Premise1: A type of school system that works well in one country will work well in any other country.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: A number of children in each country in the research sample are educated in private schools.

premise: Statement1: Climate change is caused by an increase of carbon dioxide in the earth's atmosphere. Overall, deforestation has led to the loss of half the world's trees. Statement2: Carbon dioxide traps heat and remains in the atmosphere longer than other heat-trapping gases. Statement3: Between 2000 and 2012.890,000 square miles of trees around the world were cut down -- more than one-eighth of the original forest covering earth. Statement4: Unless the rapid pace of deforestation is halted, the earth's climate will change drastically in the near future.
hypothesis: Premise1: Trees lower the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Climate change negatively impacts the earth.

premise: Statement1: Engineer : thermophotovoltaic generators are devices that convert heat into electricity. The process of manufacturing steel produces huge amounts of heat that currently go to waste. Statement2: So if steel-manufacturing plants could feed the heat they produce into thermophotovoltaic generators, they would greatly reduce their electric bills, thereby saving money.
hypothesis: Premise1: The amount steel-manufacturing plants would save on their electric bills by feeding heat into thermophotovoltaic generators would be sufficient to cover the cost of purchasing and installing those generators.

premise: Statement1: Over the last 25 years, the average price paid for a new car has steadily increased in relation to average individual income. Statement2: This increase indicates that individuals who buy new cars today spend, on average, a larger amount relative to their incomes buying a car than their counterparts did 25 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: Sales to individuals make up a smaller proportion of all new-car sales than they did 25 years ago.

premise: Statement1: Manager : one reason productivity in our office is not as high as it could be is that office workers spend too much time taking unauthorized breaks. Statement2: Since the number of office workers assigned to each manager will soon be reduced, managers will be able to supervise workers more closely in the future to make sure that they are not taking unauthorized breaks. Statement3: Therefore, productivity in our office will soon increase.

premise: Statement1: The existence of polluting industries near schools does not lead to a decrease in the air quality at those schools. Statement2: Proof of this is shown at middle school x in southern california, where an oil well was built on middle school x's campus. Statement3: The air quality at middle school x is no worse than the air quality at other middle schools in the state, and the students at middle school x do not suffer more frequently from air pollution-related allergies than do students in other middle schools in the state.
hypothesis: Premise1: More animal and plant life thrive near middle school x than at other middle schools in the state.

premise: Statement1: Lines can be parallel in a euclidean system of geometry. Statement2: But the non-euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification is regarded by several prominent physicists as correctly describing the universe we inhabit. Statement3: If these physicists are right, in our universe there are no parallel lines.
hypothesis: Premise1: There are no parallel lines in the non- euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most physicists have not doubted the view that the universe is correctly described by the non-euclidean system of geometry that has the most empirical verification.

premise: Statement1: Alan : government subsidies have been proposed in cariana to encourage farmers in rochelle, the country's principal agricultural region, to implement certain new farming techniques. Statement2: Unless these techniques are implemented, erosion of productive topsoil can not be controlled. Statement3: Unfortunately, farmers can not afford to shoulder the entire cost of the new techniques, which are more expensive than those currently used. Statement4: Therefore, without subsidies, agricultural output in rochelle will inevitably decline. Statement5: Betty : but erosion in rochelle is caused by recurring floods, which will end next year once cariana completes the hydroelectric dam it is building across the region's major river. Statement6: Therefore, rochelle's total agricultural output will stabilize at its present level even without subsidies.
hypothesis: Premise1: The current yearly output, if any, from rochelle's land that will be permanently under water once the dam is completed will at least be matched by additional yearly output from rochelle's remaining land.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The government of cariana has sufficient financial resources both to subsidize its farmers' implementation of new farming techniques and to operate a hydroelectric dam.

premise: Statement1: Bram stoker's 1897 novel dracula portrayed vampires -- the" undead" who roam at night to suck the blood of living people -- as able to turn into bats. As a result of the pervasive influence of this novel, many people now assume that a vampire's being able to turn into a bat is an essential part of vampire myths. Statement2: However, this assumption is false, for vampire myths existed in europe long before stoker's book.
hypothesis: Premise1: At least one of the european vampire myths that predated stoker's book did not portray vampires as able to turn into bats.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: At least one of the european vampire myths that predated stoker's book did not portray vampires as strictly nocturnal.

premise: Statement1: In a study, one group of volunteers was fed a high-protein, low-carbohydrate diet ; another group was fed a low-protein, high-carbohydrate diet. Both diets contained the same number of calories, and each volunteer's diet prior to the experiment had contained moderate levels of proteins and carbohydrates. Statement2: After ten days, those on the low-carbohydrate diet had lost more weight than those on the high-carbohydrate diet. Statement3: Thus, the most effective way to lose body fat is to eat much protein and shun carbohydrates.
hypothesis: Premise1: A low-protein, high-carbohydrate diet causes the human body to retain water, the added weight of which largely compensates for the weight of any body fat lost, whereas a high-protein, low-carbohydrate diet does not.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In the experiment, the volunteers on the high- carbohydrate diet engaged in regular exercise of a kind known to produce weight loss, and those on the low-carbohydrate diet did not.

premise: Statement1: It is obvious that one ought to have a will stating how one wishes one's estate to be distributed. Statement2: This can easily be seen from the fact that, according to current laws, in the absence of a legal will distant relatives whom one has never even met have a greater legal right to one's estate than one's beloved friends do.
hypothesis: Premise1: People are generally not indifferent about how their estates are distributed.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: One's beloved friends have a greater legal right to one's estate than one's distant relatives do.

premise: Statement1: Editorial : our political discussions tend to focus largely on the flaws of our nation's leaders, but we need to remind ourselves that these leaders were chosen democratically. Statement2: The real question that needs answering is how our nation's institutions and procedures enable such people to attain positions of power. Statement3: Thus, to focus our attention on the flaws of our leaders is to indulge in a pointless distraction.
hypothesis: Premise1: Examining an individual leader's personal flaws does not reveal anything about how the nation's institutions and procedures influence the selection of leaders.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: As yet, no one in the nation has made the effort to critically examine the details of the nation's institutions and procedures.

premise: Statement1: Archaeologist : neanderthals, a human-like species living 60,000 years ago, probably preserved meat by smoking it. Statement2: Burnt lichen and grass have been found in many neanderthal fireplaces. Statement3: A fire of lichen and grass produces a lot of smoke but does not produce nearly as much heat or light as a wood fire.
hypothesis: Premise1: In the region containing the neanderthal fireplaces in which lichen and grass were burnt, no plants that could be burned more effectively to produce heat or light were available 60, 000 years ago.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In close proximity to the fireplaces with lichen and grass are other fireplaces that, evidence suggests, burned material that produced more heat than smoke.

premise: Statement1: Campaigning for election to provincial or state office frequently requires that a candidate spend much time and energy catering to the interests of national party officials who can help the candidate to win office. Statement2: The elected officials who campaign for reelection while they are in office thus often fail to serve the interests of their local consistencies.
hypothesis: Premise1: Catering to the interests of national party officials sometimes conflicts with serving the interests of a provincial or state official's local constituencies.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The interests of local constituencies are well served only by elected officials who do not cater to the interests of national party officials.

premise: Statement1: Professor : economists argue that buying lottery tickets is an unwise use of resources, because the average payoff for the tickets sold in a lottery is much lower than the cost of a ticket. Statement2: But this reasoning is faulty. Statement3: The average amount paid out on individual insurance policies is much lower than the average cost of a policy, yet nobody would argue that purchasing insurance is an unwise use of resources.
hypothesis: Premise1: The protection against loss that insurance provides is more important to one's well-being than is the possibility of a windfall gain.

premise: Statement1: Commentator : in many countries the influence of fringe movements is increasing. Statement2: Scientists can thus rule out the possibility of finding a civilization as technologically advanced as our own within 50 light years of earth.

premise: Statement1: After a hepadnavirus inserts itself into a chromosome of an animal, fragments of the virus are passed on to all of that animal's descendants. Statement2: A hepadnavirus fragment is present in a chromosome of the zebra finch and in precisely the same location in a corresponding chromosome of the dark-eyed junco. Statement3: The fact that these two bird species diverged from each other about 25 million years ago therefore means that the hepadnavirus is at least 25 million years old.
hypothesis: Premise1: When a virus inserts itself into an animal's chromosome, the insertion occurs at a random spot.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Viruses can affect the evolution of an organism and can thereby influence the likelihood of their diverging into two species.

premise: Statement1: Doctor : while a few alternative medicines have dangerous side effects, some, such as many herbs, have been proven safe to consume. premise: Statement1: In an experiment, volunteers walked individually through a dark, abandoned theater. Statement2: The first half reported significantly more unusual experiences than the second did. premise: Statement1: Recently, highly skilled workers in eastern europe have left jobs in record numbers to emigrate to the west. Statement2: It is therefore likely that skilled workers who remain in eastern europe are in high demand in their home countries.

premise: Statement1: The prevailing view among historians is that medieval european peasants were deeply dedicated to religion. Statement2: But the record keepers in medieval europe were members of the clergy. premise: Statement1: Fire ants from brazil now infest the southern united states. Statement2: Unlike queen fire ants in brazil, two queens in the united states share a nest. premise: Statement1: When volcanic lava solidifies, it becomes uniformly magnetized in the direction in which the earth's magnetic field points. alt_hypothesis: Premise2: As lava flows down the side of a volcano, it picks up magnetized rocks

'sent4: s plan, omega corp. will be doing the most that can be done to cut unnecessary costs due to inefficient corporate structure.

premise: Statement1: Industrial scientists have hypothesized that much of the nitrous oxide that contributes to air pollution originates from the burning of organic matter in forest fires. premise: Statement1: Taken together, some 2,000 stocks recommended on a popular television show over the course of the past 12 years by the show's guests, most of whom are successful consultants for multibillion-dollar stock portfolios, performed less successfully than the market as a whole for this 12-year period. premise: Statement1: Economist : some sociologists argue that because capitalism intrinsically involves competition, it weakens the ties between the people of a community. premise: Statement1: With a record number of new companies starting up in derderia, and with previously established companies adding many new jobs, a record number of new jobs were created last year in the derderian economy. premise: Statement1: To prevent a newly built dam on the chiff river from blocking the route of fish migrating to breeding grounds upstream, the dam includes a fish pass, a mechanism designed to allow fish through the dam. premise: Statement1: It has been claimed that television networks should provide equal time for the presentation of opposing views whenever a television program concerns scientific issues -- such as those raised by the claims of environmentalists -- about which people disagree. Statement2: However, although an obligation to provide equal time does arise in the case of any program concerning social issues, it does so because social issues almost always have important political implications and seldom can definitely be settled on the basis of available evidence. premise: Statement1: Tissue biopsies taken on patients who have undergone throat surgery show that those who snored frequently were significantly more likely to have serious abnormalities in their throat muscles than those who snored rarely or not at all. premise: Statement1: Copyright laws protect the rights of writers to profits earned from their writings, whereas patent laws protect inventors'rights to profits earned from their inventions. In jawade, when computer-software writers demanded that their rights to profit be protected, the courts determined that information written for a machine does not fit into either the copyright or the patent category. premise: Statement1: A successful coffee stand that serves the workers in a large office building plans to increase its profits by adding oatmeal-a hot cereal popular among the workers-to its menu. premise: Statement1: Rabbits were introduced to numa island in the nineteenth century. Statement2: Overgrazing by the enormous population of rabbits now menaces the island's agriculture. The government proposes to reduce the population by using a virus that has caused devastating epidemics in rabbit populations elsewhere. premise: Statement1: The stated goal of the government's funding program for the arts is to encourage the creation of works of artistic excellence. Statement3: Senator beton concludes that government funding of the arts not only is a burden on taxpayers but also can not lead to the creation of works of true artistic excellence.

premise: Statement1: Many people suffer an allergic reaction to sulfites, including those that are commonly added to wine as preservatives. premise: Statement1: Public health experts have waged a long-standing educational campaign to get people to eat more vegetables, which are known to help prevent cancer. premise: Statement1: Scientists have shown that older bees, which usually forage outside the hive for food, tend to have larger brains than do younger bees, which usually do not forage but instead remain in the hive to tend to newly hatched bees. Statement2: Since foraging requires greater cognitive ability than does tending to newly hatched bees, it appears that foraging leads to the increased brain size of older bees.

premise: Statement1: Science writer : all scientists have beliefs and values that might slant their interpretations of the data from which they draw their conclusions. However, serious scientific papers are carefully reviewed by many other scientists before publication. Statement2: These reviewers are likely to notice and object to biases that they do not share. premise: Statement1: Many of those who are most opposed to cruelty to animals in the laboratory, in the slaughterhouse, or on the farm are people who truly love animals and who keep pets. premise: Statement1: Museum curator : sent2: our ancient egyptian collection includes an earthenware hippopotamus that resembles a child's toy. Statement3: Breaking the legs off a representation of an animal was thought to help a deceased person in this war. Statement4: We conclude that, far from being a toy, this hippopotamus was a religious object.

premise: Statement1: Art historian : successful forgeries tend to be those most recently painted. While such a phenomenon may sound counterintuitive, a forger is able to exploit current modes of aesthetics to create paintings that appeal to the eye of his or her contemporaries. hypothesis: Premise1: Those who expose the majority of paintings as forgeries are guided by aesthetic considerations.

premise: Statement1: Essayist : many social critics have claimed that modern society has become so heavily regulated by corporate and government bureaucracies that individuals lead lives over which they feel they have nearly no control. premise: Statement1: The symptoms of mental disorders are behavioral, cognitive, or emotional problems. Statement2: Some patients with mental disorders can be effectively treated with psychotherapy. premise: Statement1: Among people who live to the age of 100 or more, a large proportion have led" unhealthy" lives : smoking, consuming alcohol, eating fatty foods, and getting little exercise. premise: Statement1: A recent study found that snoring, though not common in either group, is more common among smokers than among nonsmokers. Statement2: On the basis of this evidence, the author hypothesized that smoking by itself can induce snoring.

premise: Statement1: Birds have been said to be descended from certain birdlike dinosaur species with which they share distinctive structural features. premise: Statement1: Modern flamingos derive their pink coloration from pigments stored in tiny shrimp that they filter from shallow, salty waters. The shrimp get this pigment from tiny red algae that they filter through their leg bristles. Statement2: In the jurassic period ( about 200 million years ago ), both algae and shrimp were an excellent source of food for any larger animal equipped to sieve them out of the water through an anatomical strainer. The argentine pterodactyl possessed a row of thin, bristlelike teeth through which it pumped water, straining out any tiny food particles in the process. Statement3: Thus, because it was able to filter both algae and shrimp, it is reasonable to conclude that the pterodactyl acquired a pink coloration.

premise: Statement1: The number of codfish in the north atlantic has declined substantially as the population of harp seals has increased from two million to more than three million. Some blame the seal for the shrinking cod population, but cod plays a negligible role in the seal's diet. Statement2: It is therefore unlikely that the increase in the seal population has contributed significantly to the decline in the cod population.
hypothesis: Premise1: Cod feed almost exclusively on capelin, a fish that is a staple of the harp seal's diet.

premise: Statement1: Until now, only injectable vaccines against influenza have been available. Statement2: Parents are reluctant to subject children to the pain of injections, but adults, who are at risk of serious complications from influenza, are commonly vaccinated. Statement3: A new influenza vaccine, administered painlessly in a nasal spray, is effective for children. However, since children seldom develop serious complications from influenza, no significant public health benefit would result from widespread vaccination of children using the nasal spray.
hypothesis: Premise1: Adults do not contract influenza primarily from children who have influenza.

premise: Statement1: Eight large craters run in a long straight line across a geographical region. Although some of the craters contain rocks that have undergone high-pressure shocks characteristic of meteorites slamming into earth, these shocks could also have been caused by extreme volcanic events. Statement2: Because of the linearity of the craters, it is very unlikely that some of them were caused by volcanoes and others were caused by meteorites. premise: Statement1: Carl is clearly an incompetent detective. Statement2: He has solved a smaller percentage of the cases assigned to him in the last 3 years -- only 1 out of 25 -- than any other detective on the police force.

premise: Statement1: Farmer : agricultural techniques such as crop rotation that do not use commercial products may solve agricultural problems at least as well as any technique, such as pesticide application, that does use such products. Nonetheless, no private for-profit corporation will sponsor research that is unlikely to lead to marketable products. Statement2: Thus, for the most part, only government-sponsored research investigates agricultural techniques that do not use commercial products.

premise: Statement1: Ethicist : studies have documented the capacity of placebos to reduce pain in patients who believe that they are receiving beneficial drugs. Statement2: Some doctors say that they administer placebos because medically effective treatment reinforced by the placebo effect sometimes helps patients recover faster than good treatment alone. Statement3: But administering placebos is nonetheless ethically questionable, for even if a placebo benefits a patient, a doctor might, for example, have prescribed it just to give the patient satisfaction that something was being done.
hypothesis: Premise1: The motivation for administering a placebo can be relevant to the ethical justification for doing so.

premise: Statement1: In recent years, networks of fiber-optic cable have been replacing electrical wire for transmitting telecommunications signals. Statement2: Signals running through fiber-optic cables deteriorate, and so the signals must be run through a piece of equipment called a regenerator before being transmitted farther. Light-line is the leading manufacturer of signal regenerators. Statement3: Therefore, light-line is one of the companies that will most benefit if new long-distance fiber-optic telecommunications networks are constructed.
hypothesis: Premise1: Several manufacturers are developing regenerator technologies that will allow signals to be transmitted many times farther than at present before requiring regeneration.

premise: Statement1: Red admiral butterflies fly in a highly irregular fashion, constantly varying their speed, wing strokes, and flight path. Statement2: While predators avoid poisonous butterfly species, nonpoisonous butterflies like the red admiral need to elude predators to survive. Statement3: Scientists therefore hypothesize that the red admiral's flight style, which is clearly not energy efficient, evolved as a means of avoiding predators.
hypothesis: Premise1: No species of poisonous butterfly has an irregular flight style like that of the red admiral.

premise: Statement1: Mayor of plainsville : in order to help the economy of plainsville, i am using some of our tax revenues to help bring a major highway through the town and thereby attract new business to plainsville. premise: Statement1: A survey of address changes filed with post offices and driver's license bureaus over the last ten years has established that households moving out of the city of weston outnumbered households moving into the city two to one. premise: Statement1: While the total enrollment of public elementary and secondary schools in sondland is one percent higher this academic year than last academic year, the number of teachers there increased by three percent. Statement2: Thus, the sondland education commission's prediction of a teacher shortage as early as next academic year is unfounded.
hypothesis: Premise1: A new law has mandated that there be ten percent more teachers per pupil in sondland's public schools next academic year than there were this academic year.

premise: Statement1: There are far fewer independent bookstores than there were 20 years ago, largely because chain bookstores prospered and multiplied during that time. Statement2: Thus, chain bookstores'success has been to the detriment of book consumers, for the shortage of independent bookstores has prevented the variety of readily available books from growing as much as it otherwise would have.

premise: Statement1: Ramirez : the film industry claims that pirated dvds, which are usually cheaper than legitimate dvds and become available well before a film's official dvd release date, adversely affect its bottom line. But the industry should note what the spread of piracy indicates : consumers want lower prices and faster dvd releases. Statement2: Lowering prices of dvds and releasing them sooner would mitigate piracy's negative effect on film industry profits.

premise: Statement1: Egidio's gym has been in operation for seven years, and offers regular weekly weight training and aerobic classes, as well as occasional programs in dance and martial arts. premise: Statement1: The higher the average cholesterol intake among the residents of a nation, the higher the incidence of heart disease in that nation ; the lower the average cholesterol intake, the lower the incidence of heart disease. premise: Statement1: Until now, only injectable vaccines against influenza have been available. A new vaccine administered in a nasal spray has proven effective in preventing influenza in children. Statement2: They have been used primarily by older adults, who are at risk for complications from influenza. premise: Statement1: Editorial : the roof of northtown council's equipment-storage building collapsed under the weight of last week's heavy snowfall. premise: Statement1: Most people in the united states view neither big nor small business as particularly efficient or dynamic and regard both as providing consumers with fairly priced goods and services. premise: Statement1: All oceangoing ships carry seawater ballast tanks whose weight improves stability. As a result, sea creatures often get into the tanks and are then inadvertently deposited into new habitats, where they can wreak ecological havoc. Statement3: One viable way of addressing this problem would be to empty and then immediately refill the tanks in midocean, since midocean creatures and coastal sea creatures usually can not survive in one another's habitats.

premise: Statement1: Advertisement : when your car 's engine is running at its normal operating temperature, any major brand of motor oil will protect it about as well as tuff does. premise: Statement1: Since mayor drabble always repays her political debts as soon as possible, she will almost certainly appoint lee to be the new head of the arts commission. Statement2: Lee has wanted that job for a long time, and drabble owes lee a lot for his support in the last election.

premise: Statement1: Doctor : recent pharmaceutical advances will lead the way in weight loss. premise: Statement1: Humanitarian considerations aside, sheer economics dictates that country x should institute, as country y has done, a nationwide system of air and ground transportation for conveying seriously injured persons to specialized trauma centers. premise: Statement1: After being subjected to clinical tests like those used to evaluate the effectiveness of prescription drugs, a popular nonprescription herbal remedy was found to be as effective in treating painful joints as is a certain prescription drug that has been used successfully to treat this condition. premise: Statement1: In the next decade, a large percentage of municipalities throughout the country will experience power failures if they do not modernize their electrical substations. premise: Statement1: Among the many temptations of the digital age, manipulation of photographs has proved particularly troublesome for science. premise: Statement1: Psychology researchers observed that parents feel emotion while singing to their infants. The researchers concluded that their hypothesis was correct. premise: Statement1: Though earth's human population is increasing, it currently uses only a relatively small fraction of the supply of fresh water. Statement2: Thus, claims that water shortages will plague humankind in the near future unless population growth trends change are simply mistaken.

premise: Statement1: Ordinary mountain sickness, a common condition among mountain climbers, and one from which most people can recover, is caused by the characteristic shortage of oxygen in the atmosphere at high altitudes. premise: Statement1: Start-up companies financed by venture capitalists have a much lower failure rate than companies financed by other means. We compared bone samples from european cave bears of the ice age with blood samples from present-day bears fed meat-enriched diets, and the levels of heavy nitrogen present in these samples were identical. premise: Statement1: One variety of partially biodegradable plastic beverage container is manufactured from small bits of plastic bound together by a degradable bonding agent such as cornstarch. premise: Statement1: The soaring prices of scholarly and scientific journals have forced academic libraries used only by academic researchers to drastically reduce their list of subscriptions. premise: Statement1: In wareland last year, 16 percent of licensed drivers under 21 and 11 percent of drivers ages 21-24 were in serious accidents. premise: Statement1: Mice that have been given morphine are very likely to develop blood poisoning because bacteria that normally reside in the intestine typically respond to morphine by migrating into the bloodstream. premise: Statement1: Although early jazz music featured a great deal of improvisation, this improvisation did not stray too far from the rather simple, catchy melodies upon which it was based. premise: Statement1: The more demand there is for something, the easier it is to sell. However, because there is little demand for car parts that are ten years old or older, junkyards tend not to buy those cars. premise: Statement1: Although fullerenes -- spherical molecules made entirely of carbon -- were first found in the laboratory, they have since been found in nature, formed in fissures of the rare mineral shungite. premise: Statement1: In a recent study, one group of participants watched video recordings of themselves running on treadmills, and a second group watched recordings of other people running on treadmills. premise: Statement1: Sociologist : the intended function of news is to give us information on which to act. premise: Statement1: Psychologists have found that candidates for top political offices who blink excessively during televised debates are judged by viewers to have done less well than competing candidates who exhibit average blink rates. premise: Statement1: Gartland has long been narrowly self-sufficient in both grain and meat. However, as per capita income in gartland has risen toward the world average, per capita consumption of meat has also risen toward the world average, and it takes several pounds of grain to produce one pound of meat. hypothesis: Premise1: People in gartland who increase their consumption of meat will not radically decrease their consumption of grain.

premise: Statement1: Some people think that in every barrel of politicians there are only a few rotten ones. But if deceit is a quality of rottenness, i believe all effective politicians are rotten. premise: Statement1: Agricultural societies can not exist without staple crops. premise: Statement1: The biocarb company wants to build a sterilization plant to treat contaminated medical waste in a city neighborhood where residents and environmental activists fear that such a facility will pollute the area. premise: Statement1: One of the advantages of bacillus thuringiensis ( b.t. ) Toxins over chemical insecticides results from their specificity for pest insects. hypothesis: Premise1: Insects build up resistance more readily to b. t. toxins than to chemical insecticides.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Chemical insecticides cause harm to a greater number of insect species than do b. t. toxins.

premise: Statement1: Aviation analyst : to combat human error involved in air traffic control, miro jets inc. has recently installed each of its dc-10 passenger planes with a special anti-collision device. Statement2: The device alerts the dc-10, via a red light, when another plane is slightly more than three minutes away from a potential collision. The red light comes on and stays on until the approaching plane is safely out of range. Statement3: Aviation experts at miro jet inc. have determined that three minutes is ample time for a plane to divert its respective flight path to avoid a collision. Statement4: Therefore, if the red light on the anti-collision device is off, the dc-10 is more than three minutes flying time from any plane.
hypothesis: Premise1: The anti-collision device only signals planes of oncoming directions, not those planes that are flying in parallel.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Recently, a near collision in which both planes were less than 90 seconds flying distance from each other was averted only by the prompt actions of air traffic controllers.

premise: Statement1: Surveys consistently show that the best-selling ice cream flavor is vanilla, although those who prefer chocolate rarely order vanilla. Statement2: Vanilla-flavored candy, then, probably sells better than chocolate-flavored candy.
hypothesis: Premise1: Preferences for certain ice cream flavors are similar to preferences for candy flavors.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Because someone prefers vanilla ice cream does not mean he prefers vanilla-flavored candy.

premise: Statement1: In countries in which new life-sustaining drugs can not be patented, such drugs are sold at widely affordable prices ; those same drugs, where patented, command premium prices because the patents shield patent-holding manufacturers from competitors. Statement2: These facts show that future access to new life-sustaining drugs can be improved if the practice of granting patents on newly developed life-sustaining drugs were to be abolished everywhere.
hypothesis: Premise1: Pharmaceutical companies can afford the research that goes into the development of new drugs only if patents allow them to earn high profits.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Countries that grant patents on life-sustaining drugs almost always ban their importation from countries that do not grant such patents.

premise: Statement1: Studies suggest that, for the vast majority of people who have normal blood pressure, any amount of sodium greater than that required by the body is simply excreted and does not significantly raise blood pressure. Statement2: So only persons who have high blood pressure and whose bodies are incapable of safely processing excess sodium need to restrict their sodium intake.
hypothesis: Premise1: Excess sodium intake over time often destroys the body's ability to process excess sodium.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Every human being has a physiological need for at least some sodium.

premise: Statement1: A new law gives ownership of patents-documents providing exclusive right to make and sell an invention-to universities, not the government, when those patents result from government-sponsored university research. Statement2: Administrators at logos university plan to sell any patents they acquire to corporations in order to fund programs to improve undergraduate teaching.
hypothesis: Premise1: Government-sponsored research conducted at logos university for the most part duplicates research already completed by several profit-making corporations.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Profit-making corporations interested in developing products based on patents held by universities are likely to try to serve as exclusive sponsors of ongoing university research projects.

premise: Statement1: A newly discovered painting seems to be the work of one of two 17th-century artists, either the northern german johannes drechen or the frenchman louis birelle, who sometimes painted in the same style as drechen. Statement2: Analysis of the carved picture frame, which has been identified as the painting's original 17th century frame, showed that it is made of wood found widely in northern germany at the time, but rare in the part of france where birelle lived. Statement3: This shows that the painting is most likely the work of drechen.
hypothesis: Premise1: The frame was made from wood local to the region where the picture was painted.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Sometimes a painting so closely resembles others of its era that no expert is able to confidently decide who painted it.

premise: Statement1: In ancient greece, court witnesses were not crossexamined and the jury, selected from the citizenry, received no guidance on points of law ; thus, it was extremely important for litigants to make a good impression on the jurors. Statement2: For this reason, courtroom oratory by litigants is a good source of data on the common conceptions of morality held by the citizens of ancient greece.
hypothesis: Premise1: Litigants believed jurors were likely to be impressed by litigants whose professed moral code most resembled their own.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Litigants believed jurors were more likely to be impressed by litigants whose personality they preferred.

premise: Statement1: Workers may complain about many things at work, but stress is not high on the list. Statement2: In fact, in a recent survey a majority placed boredom at the top of their list of complaints. Statement3: The assumption that job-related stress is the most serious problem for workers in the corporate world is thus simply not warranted.
hypothesis: Premise1: Workers who complain of boredom exhibit more stress-related symptoms than do those who claim their work is interesting.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Workers are less likely to complain about work if they feel that their jobs are secure.

premise: Statement1: A certain auto repair shop services a particular make of car in the downtown region of a particular city. Statement2: Three years ago, 4 % of all repairs at this shop were not successful --- the mechanics were not able to restore the car to a drivable state. This year, only 2 % of all repairs were not successful. Statement3: Clearly, the mechanics training course, given over a year ago, has considerably increased the skill of mechanics at this particular shop, so that fewer cars are beyond their ability to repair.
hypothesis: Premise1: The total number of cars served per year by this shop has increased by 35% over the past three years.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: A new shop uptown specializes in repairing cars of this make more than six years old, and most of these older cars are taken to this shop.

premise: Statement1: Ethicist : on average, animals raised on grain must be fed sixteen pounds of grain to produce one pound of meat. Statement2: A pound of meat is more nutritious for humans than a pound of grain, but sixteen pounds of grain could feed many more people than could a pound of meat. Statement3: With grain yields leveling off, large areas of farmland going out of production each year, and the population rapidly expanding, we must accept the fact that consumption of meat will soon be morally unacceptable.
hypothesis: Premise1: Often, cattle or sheep can be raised to maturity on grass from pastureland that is unsuitable for any other kind of farming.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Nutritionists agree that a diet composed solely of grain products is not adequate for human health.

premise: Statement1: Doctors in britain have long suspected that patients who wear tinted eyeglasses are abnormally prone to depression and hypochondria. Statement2: Psychological tests given there to hospital patients admitted for physical complaints like heart pain and digestive distress confirmed such a relationship. Statement3: Perhaps people whose relationship to the world is psychologically painful choose such glasses to reduce visual stimulation, which is perceived as irritating. Statement4: At any rate, it can be concluded that when such glasses are worn, it is because the wearer has a tendency to be depressed or hypochondriacal.
hypothesis: Premise1: The tinting does not dim light to the eye enough to depress the wearer's mood substantially.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: For hypochondriacs wearing tinted glasses, the glasses serve as a visual signal to others that the wearer's health is delicate.

premise: Statement1: Newspaper editorial : many pharmaceutical companies develop" me too" drugs, drugs designed to duplicate, more or less, the effect of another company's product that is already on the market. Statement2: Some critics object that such development constitutes an unnecessary, redundant use of resources that provides no new benefits to consumers. However, the entry of" me too" drugs into the market can result in a price reduction for the drugs they resemble. Statement3: Therefore, " me too" drugs can indeed benefit consumers.
hypothesis: Premise1: Some "me too" drugs turn out to be more effective than the drugs they were designed to imitate.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: If all pharmaceutical companies developed "me too" drugs, fewer innovative drugs would be developed.

premise: Statement1: The ergonomic society conducted a study that indicated that many people develop severe back problems during adulthood, and that virtually all such people who received chiropractic treatment showed great improvement. Statement2: Therefore, in order to minimize the proportion of the population that suffers from back pain, the ergonomic society recommended that chiropractic treatment be directed toward those adults who suffer from severe back problems.
hypothesis: Premise1: Individuals who receive chiropractic or other treatment prior to developing severe back problems are not less likely to develop back pain than those who do not.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Any person who receives chiropractic treatment for back pain may also benefit from other forms of treatment.

premise: Statement1: Because a large disparity in pay between the public and private sectors has developed in recent years, many experienced and extremely capable government administrators have quit their posts and taken positions in private-sector management. Statement2: Government will be able to recapture these capable administrators by raising salaries to a level comparable to those of the private sector. Statement3: In that way, the functioning of public agencies will be improved.
hypothesis: Premise1: People who moved from jobs in government administration to private-sector management would choose to change careers again.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: If the disparity in pay between government administration and private-sector management increases, administrators will move to the public sector in large numbers.

premise: Statement1: Editorialist : some people propose that, to raise revenues and encourage conservation, our country's taxes on oil, gasoline, and coal should be increased. Statement2: Such a tax increase, however, would do more harm than good. Statement3: By raising energy costs, the tax increase would decrease our competitiveness with other countries. Statement4: Many families would be unfairly burdened with higher transportation costs. Statement5: Finally, by reducing the demand for energy, the tax increase would reduce the number of energy production jobs.
hypothesis: Premise1: The proposed tax increase would be larger for some energy sources than for others.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The editorialist's country's budget deficit will decrease if the energy tax increase is implemented, thus benefiting the economy.

premise: Statement1: A drug that is highly effective in treating many types of infection can, at present, be obtained only from the bark of the ibora, a tree that is quite rare in the wild. It takes the bark of 5,000 trees to make one kilogram of the drug. Statement2: It follows, therefore, that continued production of the drug must inevitably lead to the ibora 's extinction.
hypothesis: Premise1: The ibora can be propagated from cuttings and grown under cultivation.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The drug made from ibora bark is expensive to produce.

premise: Statement1: Geologist : a geological fault in the mountain under which the proposed nuclear waste storage facility would be buried could, after a few thousand years, cause the contents to seep out or water to seep in. Since nuclear waste remains dangerous for up to 25,000 years, such seepage would be disastrous. Statement2: So we should not place a nuclear waste storage facility under this mountain until scientists investigate whether this mountain has any geological faults.
hypothesis: Premise1: The scientists' investigation would conclusively show whether or not the mountain has any geological faults.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Nuclear waste could be stored in the proposed facility on a temporary basis.

premise: Statement1: For over two centuries, no one had been able to make damascus blades-blades with a distinctive serpentine surface pattern-but a contemporary sword maker may just have rediscovered how. Statement2: Using iron with trace impurities that precisely matched those present in the iron used in historic damascus blades, this contemporary sword maker seems to have finally hit on an intricate process by which he can produce a blade indistinguishable from a true damascus blade.
hypothesis: Premise1: Production of damascus blades by sword makers of the past ceased abruptly after those sword makers' original source of iron became exhausted.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The iron with which the contemporary sword maker made damascus blades came from a source of iron that was unknown two centuries ago.

premise: Statement1: There are two supposedly conflicting hypotheses as to what makes for great national leaders : one is that such leaders successfully shape public opinion, and the other is that they are adept at reacting to it. However, treating these hypotheses as mutually exclusive is evidently a mistake. Statement2: All leaders who have had success getting their programs passed by their country's legislature have been adroit both in shaping and reacting to public opinion.
hypothesis: Premise1: Having success getting programs passed by the legislature is indicative of being a great national leader.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: It is impossible to successfully shape public opinion without in some way reacting to it.

premise: Statement1: Editor : sent2: many candidates say that if elected they will reduce governmental intrusion into voters'lives. But voters actually elect politicians who instead promise that the government will provide assistance to solve their most pressing problems. Statement2: Governmental assistance, however, costs money, and money can come only from taxes, which can be considered a form of governmental intrusion. Statement3: Thus, governmental intrusion into the lives of voters will rarely be substantially reduced over time in a democracy.
hypothesis: Premise1: Politicians who win their elections usually keep their campaign promises.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Politicians never promise what they really intend to do once in office.

premise: Statement1: Economist : technology now changes so rapidly that workers need periodic retraining. Such retraining can be efficient only if it allows individual companies to meet their own shortterm needs. Statement2: Hence, large governmental job retraining programs are no longer a viable option in the effort to retrain workers efficiently.
hypothesis: Premise1: Large governmental job retraining programs do not meet the short-term needs of individual companies.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: No single type of retraining program is most efficient at retraining technological workers.

premise: Statement1: Researchers hope to find clues about the a'mk peoples who lived in the kaumpta region about one thousand years ago but who left few obvious traces. Statement2: The researchers plan to hire the few remaining shamans of the modern-day indigenous people in kaumpta, who are believed to be descended from the a'mk, to lead them to ancestral sites that may be the remains of a'mk buildings or ceremonial spaces. Statement3: The shamans were taught the location of such sites as part of their traditional training as youths, and their knowledge of traditional kaumpta customs may help determine the nature of any sites the researchers find.
hypothesis: Premise1: The shamans have a reasonably accurate memory of a'mk sites they learned about as youths.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most or all a'mk sites are likely to be found within the kaumpta region.

premise: Statement1: Many important types of medicine have been developed from substances discovered in plants that grow only in tropical rain forests. Statement2: There are thousands of plant species in these rain forests that have not yet been studied by scientists, and it is very likely that many such plants also contain substances of medicinal value. Statement3: Thus, if the tropical rain forests are not preserved, important types of medicine will never be developed.
hypothesis: Premise1: There are substances of medicinal value contained in tropical rain forest plants not yet studied by scientists that differ from those substances already discovered in tropical rain forest plants.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Any substance of medicinal value contained in plant species indigenous to tropical rain forests will eventually be discovered if those species are studied by scientists.

premise: Statement1: Over the last five years, every new major alternative-energy initiative that initially was promised government funding has since seen that funding severely curtailed. In no such case has the government come even close to providing the level of funds initially earmarked for these projects. Statement2: Since large corporations have made it a point to discourage alternative-energy projects, it is likely that the corporations'actions influenced the government's funding decisions.
hypothesis: Premise1: The only research projects whose government funding has been severely curtailed are those that large corporations have made it a point to discourage.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The funding initially earmarked for a government project is always subject to change, given the mechanisms by which the political process operates.

premise: Statement1: Reducing stress lessens a person's sensitivity to pain. Statement2: This is the conclusion reached by researchers who played extended audiotapes to patients before they underwent surgery and afterward while they were recovering. Statement3: One tape consisted of conversation ; the other consisted of music. Statement4: Those who listened only to the latter tape required less anesthesia during surgery and fewer painkillers afterward than those who listened only to the former tape.
hypothesis: Premise1: Listening to music reduces stress.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Both anesthesia and painkillers tend to reduce stress.

premise: Statement1: There are many structural and thematic similarities between piers plowman by langland ( 1330-1400 ) and house of fame by chaucer ( 1342-1400 ), two middle english poems relating dream visions. Statement2: Some critics have argued that because a number of the shared elements are uncommon in middle english poetry, and because langland's poem probably predates chaucer's by a few years, chaucer was most likely influenced by piers plowman when writing house of fame.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many of the themes and structures of piers plowman are also found in latin, italian, french works with which chaucer could well have been familiar.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: House of fame survives in only three manuscript copies, substantially fewer than the number of manuscript copies that exist of piers plowman.

premise: Statement1: Each of two drugs, s and t, greatly reduces the effects of potentially fatal heart attacks if given as soon as possible after the attack begins, but a trial has shown that use of drug t instead of drug s would prevent death in one additional case out of 120. sent2: drug t, however, costs $ 2,000 more per treatment than drug s. sent3: therefore society is presented with a stark policy decision : whether or not to pay the $ 240,000 it would cost to use drug t in order to save one additional patient.
hypothesis: Premise1: There is no quick, practical, and relatively inexpensive way of telling for any individual case whether drug s will be as effective as drug t.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: After a heart attack, drug t remains relatively effective if given at a time at which drug s is no longer effective.

premise: Statement1: Medical researcher : scientists compared a large group of joggers who habitually stretch before jogging to an equal number of joggers who do not stretch before jogging. Statement2: Both groups of joggers incurred roughly the same number of injuries. Statement3: This indicates that stretching before jogging does not help to prevent injuries.
hypothesis: Premise1: The more prone a jogger is to jogging injuries, the more likely he or she is to develop the habit of performing stretches before jogging.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Most jogging injuries result from falls, collisions, and other mishaps on which the flexibility resulting from stretching would have little if any effect.

premise: Statement1: Medical researcher : as expected, records covering the last four years of ten major hospitals indicate that babies born prematurely were more likely to have low birth weights and to suffer from health problems than were babies not born prematurely. Statement2: These records also indicate that mothers who had received adequate prenatal care were less likely to have low birth weight babies than were mothers who had received inadequate prenatal care. Statement3: Adequate prenatal care, therefore, significantly decreases the risk of low birth weight babies.
hypothesis: Premise1: Mothers giving birth prematurely are routinely classified by hospitals as having received inadequate prenatal care when the record of that care is not available.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The hospital records indicate that many babies that are born with normal birth weights are born to mothers who had inadequate prenatal care.

premise: Statement1: Magazine article : sugar consumption may exacerbate attention deficit disorder ( add ) in children. Statement2: A recent study found that children produce large amounts of adrenaline within hours after consuming large amounts of sugar. This increase in adrenaline is especially noticeable if the source of sugar is candy, in which case the sugar's effects are not ameliorated by the ingestion of other foodstuffs.
hypothesis: Premise1: Increased adrenaline production can make add more severe in children.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Sugar consumed with food substances other than candy does not substantially increase the level of adrenaline in the bloodstream of children with add.

premise: Statement1: Traditional hatcheries raise fish in featureless environments and subject them to dull routines, whereas new, experimental hatcheries raise fish in visually stimulating environments with varied routines. When released into the wild, fish from the experimental hatcheries are bolder than those from traditional hatcheries in exploring new environments and trying new types of food. Statement2: Fish raised in the experimental hatcheries, therefore, are more likely to survive after their release.
hypothesis: Premise1: Some fish raised in traditional hatcheries die because they are too timid in their foraging for food.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Fish in the wild always live in visually stimulating environments.

premise: Statement1: People with higher-than-average blood levels of a normal dietary by-product called homocysteine are twice as likely to be diagnosed with alzheimer's disease as are those with average or below-average homocysteine levels. Statement2: Thus, it is likely that the risk of developing alzheimer's disease could be reduced by including in one's diet large amounts of b vitamins and folic acid, which convert homocysteine into substances known to have no relation to alzheimer's disease.
hypothesis: Premise1: Alzheimer's disease tends to increase the levels of homocysteine in the blood.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: B vitamins and folic acid are not metabolized by the body very efficiently when taken in the form of vitamin-mineral supplements.

premise: Statement1: In a transportation company, a certain syndrome often attributed to stress by medical experts afflicts a significantly higher percentage of workers in department f than in any other department. Statement2: We can conclude, therefore, that the work done in department f subjects workers to higher stress levels than does the work in the other departments in the company.
hypothesis: Premise1: Many workers who transfer into department f from elsewhere in the company soon begin to develop the syndrome.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Some experts believe that the syndrome can be caused by various factors, only one of which is high stress.

premise: Statement1: In mountainous regions, the timberline is the highest altitude at which trees grow. Statement2: In the rocky mountains, the current timberline is at the altitude above which growing season temperatures remain cooler than 10 degrees centigrade. Statement3: Fossilized remains of trees that grew 10,000 years ago have been found 100 meters above the current rocky mountain timberline. Statement4: Clearly, therefore, the climate of the rocky mountains is cooler now than it was 10,000 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: The fossils are not of species of trees that were able to tolerate cooler growing temperatures than are the species that currently grow near the timberline.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: In the past 10, 000 years, the only trees to have grown above today's timberline are the trees whose fossilized remains been found.

premise: Statement1: The population of peregrine falcons declined rapidly during the 1950's and 1960's and reached an all-time low in the early 1970's. Statement2: The decline was attributed by scientists to the widespread use of the pesticide ddt in rural areas.
hypothesis: Premise1: In the time since the use of ddt was banned in 1972, the population of peregrine falcons has been steadily increasing.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Peregrine falcons, like other birds of prey, abandon eggs that have fallen out of the nest, even if the eggs remain intact.

premise: Statement1: The reason j.s. Bach is remembered is not that he had a high ratio of outstanding compositions to mediocre compositions. It is rather because he was such a prolific composer. Statement2: He wrote more than a thousand full-fledged compositions, so it was inevitable that some of them would be outstanding and, being outstanding, survive the ages.
hypothesis: Premise1: Several of bach's contemporaries who produced more works than he did have been largely forgotten.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: There are a few highly regarded composers who wrote a comparatively small number of compositions.

premise: Statement1: Most small children are flat-footed. Statement2: This failure of the foot to assume its natural arch, if it persists past early childhood can sometimes result in discomfort and even pain later in life. Statement3: Traditionally, flat-footedness in children has been treated by having the children wear special shoes that give extra support to the foot, in order to foster the development of the arch.
hypothesis: Premise1: Flat-footed children who do not wear the special shoes are as likely to develop natural arches as are flat-footed children who wear the special shoes.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Although most children's flat-footedness is corrected by the time the children reach puberty, some people remain flat-footed for life.

premise: Statement1: A survey of entrepreneurs who started companies last year shows that while virtually all did substantial preparatory research and planning, only half used that work to produce a formal business plan. Statement2: Since, on average, the entrepreneurs without formal plans secured the capital they needed in half the time of those with plans, these survey results indicate that, in general, formal plans did not help the entrepreneurs who produced them to secure the capital they needed.
hypothesis: Premise1: Among the entrepreneurs surveyed, those who did not produce formal business plans sought and received a much larger proportion of their capital from investors with whom they had a long-standing business relationship.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Companies started by entrepreneurs who had used formal business plans to attract investment were on the whole as profitable in their first year as were companies started by entrepreneurs who had not produced such plans.

premise: Statement1: On the caribbean island of guadeloupe, a researcher examined 35 patients with atypical parkinson's disease and compared their eating habits to those of 65 healthy adults. Statement2: She found that all of the patients with atypical parkinson's regularly ate the tropical fruits soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle, whereas only 10 of the healthy adults regularly ate these fruits. Statement3: From this, she concluded that eating these fruits causes atypical parkinson's.
hypothesis: Premise1: For many of the atypical parkinson's patients, their symptoms stopped getting worse, and in some cases actually abated, when they stopped eating soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The 10 healthy adults who regularly ate soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle ate significantly greater quantities of these fruits, on average, than did the 35 atypical parkinson's patients.

premise: Statement1: In the nation of partoria, large trucks currently have a much higher rate of traffic accidents per mile driven than other vehicles do. Statement2: However, the very largest trucks-those with three trailers-had less than a third of the accident rate of single- and double-trailer trucks. Statement3: Clearly, therefore, one way for partoria to reduce the number of traffic accidents would be to require shippers to increase their use of triple-trailer trucks.
hypothesis: Premise1: Partorian trucking companies currently use triple-trailer trucks only for long trips using major highways, which is the safest kind of trip for large trucks.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: No matter what changes partoria makes in the regulation of trucking, it will have to keep some smaller roads off-limits to all large trucks.

premise: Statement1: Gilbert : this food label is mistaken. It says that these cookies contain only natural ingredients, but they contain alphahydroxy acids that are chemically synthesized by the cookie company at their plant. Statement2: Sabina : the label is not mistaken. Statement3: After all, alphahydroxy acids also are found occurring naturally in sugarcane.
hypothesis: Premise1: All substances except those that do not occur naturally in any source are considered natural.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Not all chemicals that are part of the manufacturing process are ingredients of the cookies.

premise: Statement1: Feathers recently taken from seabirds stuffed and preserved in the 1880s have been found to contain only half as much mercury as feathers recently taken from living birds of the same species. Statement2: Since mercury that accumulates in a seabird's feathers as the feathers grow is derived from fish eaten by the bird, these results indicate that mercury levels in saltwater fish are higher now than they were 100 years ago.
hypothesis: Premise1: The process used to preserve birds in the 1880s did not substantially decrease the amount of mercury in the birds' feathers
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The amount of mercury in a saltwater fish depends on the amount of pollution in the ocean habitat of the fish

premise: Statement1: Caffeine can kill or inhibit the growth of the larvae of several species of insects. Statement2: One recent experiment showed that tobacco hornworm larvae die when they ingest a preparation that consists, in part, of finely powdered tea leaves, which contain caffeine. Statement3: This result is evidence for the hypothesis that the presence of non-negligible quantities of caffeine in various parts of many diverse species of plants is not accidental but evolved as a defense for those plants.
hypothesis: Premise1: Caffeine-producing plants or their ancestors have at some time been subject to being fed upon by creatures sensitive to caffeine
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: The tobacco plant is among the plant species that produce caffeine for their own defense

premise: Statement1: Political advertisement : mayor delmont's critics complain about the jobs that were lost in the city under delmont's leadership. Yet the fact is that not only were more jobs created than were eliminated, but the average pay for these new jobs has been higher than the average pay for jobs citywide every year since delmont took office. Statement2: So there can be no question that throughout delmont's tenure the average paycheck in this city has been getting steadily bigger.
hypothesis: Premise1: The average pay for jobs eliminated in the city during mayor delmont's tenure has been roughly equal every year to the average pay for jobs citywide.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Average pay in the city was at a ten-year low when mayor delmont took office.

premise: Statement1: Government official : currently many business travelers rely on a one-hour flight between the cities, but complain about congestion and delays at the airport. Given this option, many who fly between the two cities during the week will choose the railway, since it will allow them to travel between the city centers in less time than a flight with the expected delays plus the commute to and from airports would take. Statement2: The new high-speed railway between hamport and dudley, aplandia 's two major financial hubs, will be able to make the same trip in slightly less than three hours.
hypothesis: Premise1: The proposed speed at which trains are to travel will produce static electricity that will interfere with other trains' equipment, thereby causing significant delays.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Twice within the last two decades there have been significant recessions in aplandia.

premise: Statement1: If the government increases its funding for civilian scientific research, private patrons and industries will believe that such research has become primarily the government's responsibility. Statement2: When they believe that research is no longer primarily their responsibility, private patrons and industries will decrease their contributions toward research. Statement3: Therefore, in order to keep from depressing the overall level of funding for civilian scientific research, the government should not increase its own funding.
hypothesis: Premise1: Any increase in government funding would displace more private funding for funding for civilian scientific research than it would provide.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: Funding for civilian scientific research is currently at the highest possible level.

premise: Statement1: Chronic fatigue syndrome is characterized by prolonged fatigue, muscular pain, and neurological problems. Statement2: It is not known whether these symptoms are all caused by a single virus or whether each symptom is the result of a separate viral infection. Statement3: A newly synthesized drug has been tested on those who suffer from chronic fatigue syndrome. Although the specific antiviral effects of this drug are unknown, it has lessened the severity of all of the symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome. Statement4: Thus there is evidence that chronic fatigue syndrome is, in fact, caused by one virus.
hypothesis: Premise1: It is more likely that the new drug counteracts one virus than that it counteracts several viruses.
alt_hypothesis: Premise2: All those who suffer from prolonged fatigue also suffer from neurological problems.

premise: Statement1: An overwhelming proportion of the most productive employees at saleco's regional offices work not eight hours a day, five days a week, as do other saleco employees, but rather ten hours a day, four days a week, with friday off. Statement2: Noting this phenomenon, saleco's president plans to increase overall productivity by keeping the offices closed on fridays and having all employees work the same schedule-ten hours a day, four days a week.
hypothesis: Premise1: Working ten hours a day has allowed the most productive employees to work two hours alone each day in their respective offices relatively undisturbed by fellow employees.

premise: Statement1: A study of 86 patients, all of whom suffered from disease t and received the same standard medical treatment, divided the patients into 2 equal groups. premise: Statement1: Sleep research has demonstrated that sleep is characterized by periods of different levels of brain activity. premise: Statement1: Earthworms, vital to the health of soil, prefer soil that is approximately neutral on the acid-to-alkaline scale. premise: Statement1: More and more law firms specializing in corporate taxes are paid on a contingency-fee basis. premise: Statement1: It is popularly believed that a poem has whatever meaning is assigned to it by the reader. premise: Statement1: Twelve years ago and again five years ago, there were extended periods when the darfir republic's currency, the pundra, was weak : its value was unusually low relative to the world's most stable currencies. premise: Statement1: Physiological research has uncovered disturbing evidence linking a number of structural disorders to jogging. hypothesis: Premise1: The link between jogging and certain structural disorders appears to be a causal one.

premise: Sent1:" life expectancy" is the average age at death of the entire live-born population. premise: Statement1: Some political thinkers hope to devise a form of government in which every citizen's rights are respected. But such a form of government is impossible. premise: Statement1: Guidebook writer : i have visited hotels throughout the country and have noticed that in those built before 1930 the quality of the original carpentry work is generally superior to that in hotels built afterward. premise: Statement1: It has been said that authors who write in order to give pleasure can not impart to their readers the truth of their subject matter. That claim can not be true.
